translations.text: who prevent others from the way of God, seek to make it appear crooked, and who have no faith in the life hereafter.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Who turn away from the path of Allah and desire to make it crooked; and they are disbelievers in the hereafter.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Such as those who turn others away from the path of God and seek to make it appear crooked: these are the ones who deny the Hereafter.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Those who would hinder (men) from the path of Allah and would seek in it something crooked: these were they who denied the Hereafter!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena yasuddoona AAan sabeeli Allahi wayabghoonaha AAiwajan wahum bialakhirati hum kafiroona

20.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ مُعْجِزِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا كَانَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۘ يُضَـٰعَفُ لَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۚ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ يُبْصِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They shall not weaken (the power of) God on earth, nor find any other protector but God. Their punishment will be doubled, for they could neither hear nor see.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will not be able to escape in the earth, nor do they have any protecting friends apart from Allah; they will have punishment upon punishment; they were unable to hear, nor used to see. (* Hear or see the truth.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: they are unable to frustrate Him on earth and they have no protectors, apart from God. For them the chastisement shall be doubled; they could not hear, neither did they see.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: These could not escape on the earth, nor could there be for them protectors against Allah; doubled shall be the torment for them; they were notable to hearken, nor would they be clear sighted.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: By no means will they escape (from Allah's Torment) on earth, nor have they protectors besides Allah! Their torment will be doubled! They could not bear to hear (the preachers of the truth) and they used not to see (the truth because of their severe aversin, inspite of the fact that they had the sense of hearing and sight).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: These will not escape on earth, and they have no protectors besides God. The punishment will be doubled for them. They have failed to hear, and they have failed to see.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They had no power to frustrate Allah's design in the earth, nor did they have any protectors against Allah. Their chastisement will be doubled. They were unable to hear, nor could they see.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: By no means will they escape on earth, nor have they protectors besides Allah! Their torment will be doubled! They could not bear to listen and they used not to see (the truth).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Such will not escape in the earth, nor have they any protecting friends beside Allah. For them the torment will be double. They could not bear to hear, and they used not to see.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They cannot frustrate [Allah] on the earth, nor do they have besides Allah any guardian. For them the punishment shall be doubled, for they could neither listen, nor did they use to see.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They are unable to frustrate Him on the earth; there is none to protect them, except Allah. Those, their punishment shall be doubled; they could neither hear nor see.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those were not causing failure [to Allah] on earth, nor did they have besides Allah any protectors. For them the punishment will be multiplied. They were not able to hear, nor did they see.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Such people will never weaken God's (power) on earth nor will they find any guardian besides God. Their punishment will be doubled and they will not be able to hear or see.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These shall not escape in the earth, nor shall they have any guardians besides Allah; the punishment shall be doubled for them, they could not bear to hear and they did not see.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They can never frustrate God on earth, nor have they any protectors besides God. They will be subjected to double punishment, for they could neither hear nor see.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will in no wise frustrate (His design) on earth, nor have they protectors besides Allah! Their penalty will be doubled! They lost the power to hear, and they did not see!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika lam yakoonoo muAAjizeena fee alardi wama kana lahum min dooni Allahi min awliyaa yudaAAafu lahumu alAAathabu ma kanoo yastateeAAoona alssamAAa wama kanoo yubsiroona

21.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They are verily those who exceeded themselves, and the (gods) they invented abandoned them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is they who put their souls into ruin, and they lost all that they used to fabricate.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those are they that have lost their souls, and that they forged has gone astray from them;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: These are they who have lost their souls, and hath strayed from them that which they have been fabricating.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They are those who have lost their ownselves, and their invented false deities will vanish from them.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those are the ones who lost their souls, and what they had invented has strayed away from them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They caused utter loss to themselves, and all that they had invented failed them.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They are those who have lost their own selves, and that which they invented eluded them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Such are they who have lost their souls, and that which they used to invent hath failed them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They are the ones who have ruined their souls, and what they used to fabricate has forsaken them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those are they that have lost their souls, and that which they forged has left them;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those are the ones who will have lost themselves, and lost from them is what they used to invent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They have lost their souls and their false deities will turn away from them.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These are they who have lost their souls, and what they forged is gone from them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: It is such as these who have ruined their souls, and that which they fabricated shall fail them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They are the ones who have lost their own souls: and the (fancies) they invented have left them in the lurch!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika allatheena khasiroo anfusahum wadalla AAanhum ma kanoo yaftaroona

22.arabic: لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمُ ٱلْأَخْسَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Undoubtedly they will be losers in the life to come.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will be, unnecessarily, the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: they without doubt will be the greatest losers in the world to come.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Undoubtedly they! in the Hereafter they shall be the greatest losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Certainly, they are those who will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Without a doubt, in the Hereafter, they will be the biggest losers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Doubtlessly, they shall be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Certainly, they are those who will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Assuredly in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Undoubtedly, they are the ones who will be the biggest losers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: without doubt they will be the greatest losers in the Everlasting Life.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Assuredly, it is they in the Hereafter who will be the greatest losers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: In the life to come they will certainly lose a great deal.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Truly in the hereafter they are the greatest losers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: In the Hereafter, it is they who shall be the greatest losers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Without a doubt, these are the very ones who will lose most in the Hereafter!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: La jarama annahum fee alakhirati humu alakhsaroona

23.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَأَخْبَتُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But those who believe and do things good, and are humble before their Lord, are men of Paradise where they will abide for ever.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who believed and performed good deeds and directed themselves towards their Lord – they are the people of Paradise; they will abide in it forever.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But those who believe, and do righteous deeds, and have humbled themselves unto their Lord -- they shall be the inhabitants of Paradise, therein dwelling forever.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who believed and worked righteous works and humbled themselves before their Lord--they shall be the fellows of the Garden: therein they shall be abiders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah - Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, and humble themselves (in repentance and obedience) before their Lord, - they will be dwellers of Paradise to dwell therein forever.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who believe and do good deeds, and humble themselves before their Lord—these are the inhabitants of Paradise, where they will abide forever.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those who believed and acted righteously and dedicated themselves totally to their Lord -they are the people of Paradise, and there they shall abide forever.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and humble themselves before their Lord, they will be the dwellers of Paradise to dwell therein forever.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who believe and do good works and humble themselves before their Lord: such are rightful owners of the Garden; they will abide therein.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds and are humble before their Lord—they shall be the inhabitants of paradise, and they shall remain in it [forever].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Whereas those who believe and do good deeds and humble themselves before their Lord, they are the companions of Paradise, and there they shall live for ever.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, they who have believed and done righteous deeds and humbled themselves to their Lord - those are the companions of Paradise; they will abide eternally therein.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The righteously striving believers who are humble before their Lord, will be the dwellers of Paradise wherein they will live forever.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely (as to) those who believe and do good and humble themselves to their Lord, these are the dwellers of the garden, in it they will abide.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who have believed and done good deeds and humbled themselves before their Lord are destined for Paradise, and they will live in it forever.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But those who believe and work righteousness, and humble themselves before their Lord,- They will be companions of the gardens, to dwell therein for aye!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alsalihati waakhbatoo ila rabbihim olaika ashabu aljannati hum feeha khalidoona

24.arabic: مَثَلُ ٱلْفَرِيقَيْنِ كَٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْأَصَمِّ وَٱلْبَصِيرِ وَٱلسَّمِيعِ ۚ هَلْ يَسْتَوِيَانِ مَثَلًا ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The semblance of these two groups is that of a man who is deaf and blind, and the other who can hear and see. Can they be equal? Why do you not reflect?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The example of the two groups is like one being blind and deaf, and the other seeing and hearing; are they equal in condition? So do you not ponder?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The likeness of the two parties is as the man blind and deaf, and the man who sees and hears; are they equal in likeness? Will you not remember?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The likeness of the two parties is as the blind and deaf, and the seeing and hearing. Are the twain equal in likeness? Admonished are ye not then?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The likeness of the two parties is as the blind and the deaf and the seer and the hearer. Are they equal when compared? Will you not then take heed?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The parable of the two groups is that of the blind and the deaf, and the seeing and the hearing. Are they equal in comparison? Will you not reflect?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The example of the two parties is that one is blind and deaf, and the other capable of seeing and hearing. Can the two be equals? Will you, then, not heed?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The likeness of the two parties is as the blind and the deaf and the seer and the hearer. Are they equal when compared Will you not then take heed

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The similitude of the two parties is as the blind and the deaf and the seer and the hearer. Are they equal in similitude? Will ye not then be admonished?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The parable of the two parties is that of one who is blind and deaf and one who sees and hears. Are they equal in comparison? Will you not then take admonition?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The likeness of the two parties is as one who is blind and deaf, and one that sees and hears, are they equally alike, will you not remember?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The example of the two parties is like the blind and deaf, and the seeing and hearing. Are they equal in comparison? Then, will you not remember?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Can the two groups, the blind and the deaf, be considered equal to those who have vision and hearing? Will you then not take heed?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The likeness of the two parties is as the blind and the deaf and the seeing and the hearing: are they equal in condition? Will you not then mind?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: These two groups are like the blind and the deaf as compared with those who can see and hear. Can the two be equal? Will you not then understand?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: These two kinds (of men) may be compared to the blind and deaf, and those who can see and hear well. Are they equal when compared? Will ye not then take heed?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Mathalu alfareeqayni kaalaAAma waalasammi waalbaseeri waalssameeAAi hal yastawiyani mathalan afala tathakkaroona

25.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنِّى لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people (and he said): "I give you a clear warning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed We sent Nooh to his people that, “Indeed I am for you a clear Herald of Warning.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We sent Noah to his people: 'I am for you a warner, and a bearer of good tidings:

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people saying: verily I am unto you a plain warner.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people (and he said): "I have come to you as a plain warner."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people, “I am to you a clear warner.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Such were the circumstances) when We sent forth Noah to his people. (He said): 'I have been sent to you to warn you plainly

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed We sent Nuh to his people (and he said): "I have come to you as a plain warner."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We sent Noah unto his folk (and he said): Lo! I am a plain warner unto you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people [to declare]: ‘Indeed I am a manifest warner to you.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We sent Noah to his nation. (He said:) 'I am a warner for you, and a bearer of glad tidings.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We had certainly sent Noah to his people, [saying], "Indeed, I am to you a clear warner

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people to give them the clear warning

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly We sent Nuh to his people: Surely I am a plain warner for you:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, "I have come to you with a clear warning:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people (with a mission): "I have come to you with a Clear Warning:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad arsalna noohan ila qawmihi innee lakum natheerun mubeenun

26.arabic: أَن لَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ أَلِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not worship anyone but God; for I fear the punishment of a dreadful day for you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “That you must worship none except Allah; indeed I fear the punishment of the calamitous day upon you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Serve you none but God. I fear for you the chastisement of a painful day.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That ye shall worship none except Allah; verily I fear for you the torment of a Day afflictive.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "That you worship none but Allah, surely, I fear for you the torment of a painful Day."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “That you shall worship none but God. I fear for you the agony of a painful Day.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: that you may worship none but Allah or else I fear for you the chastisement of a Grievous Day.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "That you worship none but Allah; surely, I fear for you the torment of a painful Day."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That ye serve none, save Allah. Lo! I fear for you the retribution of a painful Day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Worship none but Allah. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Worship none except Allah. I fear for you the punishment of a painful Day'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That you not worship except Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a painful day."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: that they should not worship anyone besides God. (Noah warned them), "I am afraid that you will suffer the most painful torment".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That you shall not serve any but Allah, surely I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: worship none but God. I fear lest punishment befall you on a woeful Day."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "That ye serve none but Allah: Verily I do fear for you the penalty of a grievous day."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: An la taAAbudoo illa Allaha innee akhafu AAalaykum AAathaba yawmin aleemin

27.arabic: فَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ مَا نَرَىٰكَ إِلَّا بَشَرًا مِّثْلَنَا وَمَا نَرَىٰكَ ٱتَّبَعَكَ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ أَرَاذِلُنَا بَادِىَ ٱلرَّأْىِ وَمَا نَرَىٰ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا مِن فَضْلٍۭ بَلْ نَظُنُّكُمْ كَـٰذِبِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The leaders of the people who were unbelievers, replied: "We see that you are but a man like us, and see that none among us follows you but the meanest and immature of judgement, and do not see any excellence in you above us. In fact, we think you are a liar."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The chiefs of his people, who were disbelievers, said, “We see that you are just a mortal like us, and we do not see anyone following you except the most lowly among us, without insight; and we do not find any merit in you above us – in fact, we consider you liars.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the Council of the unbelievers of his people, 'We see thee not other than a mortal like ourselves, and we see no? any following thee but the vilest of us, inconsiderately. We do not see you have over us any superiority; no, rather we think you are liars.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The chiefs of those who disbelieved among his people said: we behold thee except as a human being like us, and we behold not any follow thee except the meanest of us, by an immature opinion; nor We behold in you any exellency over us; nay! we deem you liars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The chiefs of the disbelievers among his people said: "We see you but a man like ourselves, nor do we see any follow you but the meanest among us and they (too) followed you without thinking. And we do not see in you any merit above us, in fact we think you are liars."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The notables who disbelieved among his people said, “We see in you nothing but a man like us, and we see that only the worst among us have followed you, those of immature judgment. And we see that you have no advantage over us. In fact, we think you are liars.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The notables among Noah's own people, who had refused to follow him, responded: 'We merely consider you a human being like ourselves. Nor do we find among those who follow you except the lowliest of our folk, the men who follow you without any proper reason. We see nothing in you to suggest that you are any better than us. Rather, we believe you to be liars.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The chiefs who disbelieved among his people said: "We see you but a man like ourselves, nor do we see any follow you but the meanest among us and they (too) followed you without thinking. And we do not see in you any merit above us, in fact we think you are liars."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The chieftains of his folk, who disbelieved, said: We see thee but a mortal like us, and we see not that any follow thee save the most abject among us, without reflection. We behold in you no merit above us - nay, we deem you liars.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But the elite of the faithless from among his people said, ‘We do not see you to be anything but a human being like ourselves, and we do not see anyone following you except those who are simpleminded riffraff from our midst. Nor do we see that you have any merit over us. Indeed, we consider you to be liars.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The unbelieving Council of his nation said: 'We do not see you other than a human like ourselves. We see your followers are none but the lowliest amongst us, and their opinion is not to be considered. We do not see you superior to us, rather, we consider you liars'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said, "We do not see you but as a man like ourselves, and we do not see you followed except by those who are the lowest of us [and] at first suggestion. And we do not see in you over us any merit; rather, we think you are liars."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The disbelievers among his people said, "We do not believe that you are any better than the rest of us; we see that only the worthless hasty ones, the lowliest among us follow you. Thus, we do not think that you are superior to us, rather you are all liars."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But the chiefs of those who disbelieved from among his people said: We do not consider you but a mortal like ourselves, and we do not see any have followed you but those who are the meanest of us at first thought and we do not see in you any excellence over us; nay, we deem you liars.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The leaders of his people, who refused to acknowledge the truth, said, "We regard you only as a human being like ourselves. We do not see that anyone follows you but the lowliest of us, those of immature judgement. We see no superior merit in you; in fact we believe you are a liar."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But the chiefs of the Unbelievers among his people said: "We see (in) thee nothing but a man like ourselves: Nor do we see that any follow thee but the meanest among us, in judgment immature: Nor do we see in you (all) any merit above us: in fact we think ye are liars!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faqala almalao allatheena kafaroo min qawmihi ma naraka illa basharan mithlana wama naraka ittabaAAaka illa allatheena hum arathiluna badiya alrrayi wama nara lakum AAalayna min fadlin bal nathunnukum kathibeena

28.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَءَاتَىٰنِى رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِهِۦ فَعُمِّيَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنُلْزِمُكُمُوهَا وَأَنتُمْ لَهَا كَـٰرِهُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people, think. If I have a clear proof from my Lord, and He has bestowed on me His grace, though unknown to you, can we force it upon you when you are averse?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O my people! What is your opinion – if I am on a clear proof from my Lord and He has bestowed mercy upon me from Him, so you remained blind towards it; shall we force it upon you although you dislike it?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'O my people, what think you? If I stand upon a clear sign from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Him, and it has been obscured for you, shall we compel you to it while you are averse to it?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: bethink ye, O my people! if I rested upon an evidence from my Lord, and a mercy hath come unto me from Him, and that hath been obscured unto you, Shall we make you adhere to it while ye are averse thereto?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear proof from my Lord, and a Mercy (Prophethood, etc.) has come to me from Him, but that (Mercy) has been obscured from your sight. Shall we compel you to accept it (Islamic Monotheism) when you have a strong hatred for it?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O my people, Have you considered? If I stand on clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me a mercy from Himself, but you were blind to it, can we compel you to accept it, even though you dislike it?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Noah said: 'My people! If I base myself on a clear evidence from my Lord, and I have also been blessed by His mercy to which you have been blind, how can we force it upon you despite your aversion to it?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear proof from my Lord, and a mercy (prophethood) has come to me from Him, but that (mercy) has been obscured from your sight. Shall we compel you (to accept) it when you have a strong hatred for it

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O my people! Bethink you, if I rely on a clear proof from my Lord and there hath come unto me a mercy from His presence, and it hath been made obscure to you, can we compel you to accept it when ye are averse thereto?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O my people! Tell me, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and He has granted me His own mercy—though it should be invisible to you—shall we force it upon you while you are averse to it?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He said: 'What do you think my nation! If I have a clear proof from my Lord and He has given me mercy from Him, though it is hidden from you, can wecompel you to accept it when you hate it?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "O my people have you considered: if I should be upon clear evidence from my Lord while He has given me mercy from Himself but it has been made unapparent to you, should we force it upon you while you are averse to it?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Noah replied, "My people do you think - that if my Lord has sent me a miracle and granted me mercy but your ignorance has obscured them from your sight - we can force you to believe when you do not want to?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O my people! tell me if I have with me clear proof from my Lord, and He has granted me mercy from Himself and it has been made obscure to you; shall we constrain you to (accept) it while you are averse from it?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "O my people, tell me: if I have clear evidence from my Lord and He has favoured me with grace from Himself, which you have been unable to recognize, can we force it on you against your will?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people! See ye if (it be that) I have a Clear Sign from my Lord, and that He hath sent Mercy unto me from His own presence, but that the Mercy hath been obscured from your sight? shall we compel you to accept it when ye are averse to it?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya qawmi araaytum in kuntu AAala bayyinatin min rabbee waatanee rahmatan min AAindihi faAAummiyat AAalaykum anulzimukumooha waantum laha karihoona

29.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ لَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مَالًا ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ بِطَارِدِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَـٰكِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُمْ قَوْمًا تَجْهَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I do not demand for it any wealth from you, O my people. My reward is with God. And I will not drive those away who believe. They have also to meet their Lord. But I see you are an ignorant people.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And O my people! I do not ask any wealth from you for it; my reward is only upon Allah, and I am not going to repel the Muslims; indeed they will meet their Lord, but I find you people completely ignorant.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O my people, I do not ask of you wealth for this; my wage falls only upon God. I will not drive away those who believe; they shall surely meet their Lord. But I see you are an ignorant people.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And, O my people, I ask not of you any riches therefor; my hire is not but with Allah. And I am not going to drive away those who have believed; verily they are going to meet their Lord; but I behold you a people ignorant!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And O my people! I ask of you no wealth for it, my reward is from none but Allah. I am not going to drive away those who have believed. Surely, they are going to meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people that are ignorant.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O my people! I ask of you no money for it. My reward lies only with God. And I am not about to dismiss those who believed; they will surely meet their Lord. And I see that you are ignorant people.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My people! I seek no recompense from you. My recompense is only with Allah. Nor will I drive away those who believe. They are destined to meet their Lord. But I find you to be an ignorant people.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And O my people! I ask of you no wealth for it, my reward is from none but Allah. I am not going to drive away those who have believed. Surely, they are going to meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people that are ignorant.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And O my people! I ask of you no wealth therefor. My reward is the concern only of Allah, and I am not going to thrust away those who believe - Lo! they have to meet their Lord! - but I see you a folk that are ignorant.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O my people! I do not ask you any material reward for it. My reward lies only with Allah. But I will not drive away those who have faith: they will indeed encounter their Lord. But I see that you are an ignorant lot.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: My nation, for this I do not ask you for your wealth for my wage is only with Allah. Nor will I drive away the believers, for they will surely meet their Lord. But, I can see that you are ignorant.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And O my people, I ask not of you for it any wealth. My reward is not but from Allah. And I am not one to drive away those who have believed. Indeed, they will meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people behaving ignorantly.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My people I do not ask any payment for what I preach to you. No one except God has to give me any reward. I do not drive away those who have faith (in my teaching); they will all receive mercy from their Lord. I know that you are ignorant people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And, O my people! I ask you not for wealth in return for it; my reward is only with Allah and I am not going to drive away those who believe; surely they shall meet their Lord, but I consider you a people who are ignorant:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: O my people, I do not ask you for any money for this; my reward comes only from God. I will not drive away those who believe; they shall surely meet their Lord. Yet I see that you are a people who act out of ignorance.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And O my people! I ask you for no wealth in return: my reward is from none but Allah: But I will not drive away (in contempt) those who believe: for verily they are to meet their Lord, and ye I see are the ignorant ones!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waya qawmi la asalukum AAalayhi malan in ajriya illa AAala Allahi wama ana bitaridi allatheena amanoo innahum mulaqoo rabbihim walakinnee arakum qawman tajhaloona

30.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ مَن يَنصُرُنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِن طَرَدتُّهُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O my people, who will save me from God if I drive them away? Do you not understand?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And O my people! Who would rescue me from Allah if I repel them? So do you not think?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O my people, who would help me against God, if I drive you away?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O My people! who will succour me against Allah, if I drave them away! Admonished are ye not then?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And O my people! Who will help me against Allah, if I drove them away? Will you not then give a thought?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O my people! Who will support me against God, if I dismiss them? Will you not give a thought?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My people! Were I to drive the men of faith away, who will protect me from (the chastisement of) Allah? Do you not understand even this much?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And O my people! Who will help me against Allah, if I drove them away Will you not then give a thought

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And, O my people! who would deliver me from Allah if I thrust them away? Will ye not then reflect?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O my people! Who would come to my help against Allah were I to drive them away? Will you not then take admonition?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Were I to drive you away my nation, who would help me from Allah? Will you not remember?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And O my people, who would protect me from Allah if I drove them away? Then will you not be reminded?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My people, who would protect me against God if I were to drive these people away? Will you then not take heed?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And, O my people! who will help me against Allah if I drive them away? Will you not then mind?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: My people, who would support  me against God if I were to drive them off? Will you not take heed?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And O my people! who would help me against Allah if I drove them away? Will ye not then take heed?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waya qawmi man yansurunee mina Allahi in taradtuhum afala tathakkaroona

31.arabic: وَلَآ أَقُولُ لَكُمْ عِندِى خَزَآئِنُ ٱللَّهِ وَلَآ أَعْلَمُ ٱلْغَيْبَ وَلَآ أَقُولُ إِنِّى مَلَكٌ وَلَآ أَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ تَزْدَرِىٓ أَعْيُنُكُمْ لَن يُؤْتِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ خَيْرًا ۖ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ إِنِّىٓ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I say not that I have the treasures of God, or that I possess the knowledge of the unknown. I do not claim to be an angel, nor can I say that God will not bestow any good on those you disdain, for God is cognisant of what is in their hearts. If I say this, I will surely be unjust."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And I do not say to you, ‘I have the treasures of Allah’ nor that ‘I gain knowledge of the hidden’, nor do I say that, ‘I am an angel’ nor do I say to those whom your sights consider lowly, that Allah will never give them any good; Allah well knows what is in their hearts; if I do so, I would then be of the unjust.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Will you not remember? I do not say to you, "I possess the treasuries of God"; I know not the Unseen; and I do not say, "I am an angel." Nor do I say to those your eyes despise, "God will not give them any good"; God knows best what is in their hearts. 'Surely in that case I should be among the evildoers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And I say not unto you that with me are the treasures of Allah, nor that I know the Unseen; nor I say, I am an angel. And say not of those whom your eyes condemn that Allah shall not bestow on them good - -Allah knoweth best that which is in theeir souls. Verily in that case would be of the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And I do not say to you that with me are the Treasures of Allah, "Nor that I know the Ghaib (unseen); "nor do I say I am an angel, and I do not say of those whom your eyes look down upon that Allah will not bestow any good on them. Allah knows what is in their inner-selves (as regards belief, etc.). In that case, I should, indeed be one of the Zalimun (wrong-doers, oppressors, etc.)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “I do not say to you that I possess the treasures of God, nor do I know the future, nor do I say that I am an angel. Nor do I say of those who are despicable in your eyes that God will never give them any good. God is Aware of what lies in their souls. If I did, I would be one of the wrongdoers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: I do not say to you that I possess Allah's treasures, nor that I have access to the realm beyond the ken of sense-perception, nor do I claim to be an angel. Nor do I say regarding those whom you look upon with disdain that Allah will not bestow any good upon them. Allah knows best what is in their hearts. Were I to say so I would be one of the wrongdoers.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And I do not say to you that with me are the treasures of Allah, nor that I know the Ghayb; nor do I say I am an angel, and I do not say of those whom your eyes look down upon that Allah will not bestow any good on them. Allah knows what is in their inner selves. In that case, I should, indeed be one of the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: I say not unto you: "I have the treasures of Allah" nor "I have knowledge of the Unseen," nor say I: "Lo! I am an angel!" Nor say I unto those whom your eyes scorn that Allah will not give them good - Allah knoweth best what is in their hearts - Lo! then indeed I should be of the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: I do not say to you that I possess the treasuries of Allah, neither do I know the Unseen. I do not claim to be an angel, neither do I say of those who are despicable in your eyes that Allah will not grant them any good—Allah knows best what is in their hearts—for then I would indeed be a wrongdoer.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: I do not say to you that I possess the treasuries of Allah, and I do not know the unseen. I do not say I am an angel, nor do I say to those whom you despise, Allah will not give them any good. Allah knows best what is in their hearts. Indeed, if this were so then I would be amongst the harmdoers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And I do not tell you that I have the depositories [containing the provision] of Allah or that I know the unseen, nor do I tell you that I am an angel, nor do I say of those upon whom your eyes look down that Allah will never grant them any good. Allah is most knowing of what is within their souls. Indeed, I would then be among the wrongdoers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: I do not say that God's treasures belong to me, that I know the unseen, or that I am an angel. Nor do I say about those whom you disdain that God will not give them any reward. God knows best what is in their hearts, for then I would be unjust."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And I do not say to you that I have the treasures of Allah and I do not know the unseen, nor do I say that I am an angel, nor do I say about those whom your eyes hold in mean estimation (that) Allah will never grant them (any) good-- Allah knows best what is in their souls-- for then most surely I should be of the unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: I do not say to you that I possess God's treasures, or that I have knowledge of the unseen, or that I am an angel. Nor do I say concerning those upon whom you look with contempt, that God will not bestow any good upon them -- God knows best what is in their hearts. If I did, I would certainly be one of the wrongdoers."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "I tell you not that with me are the treasures of Allah, nor do I know what is hidden, nor claim I to be an angel. Nor yet do I say, of those whom your eyes do despise that Allah will not grant them (all) that is good: Allah knoweth best what is in their souls: I should, if I did, indeed be a wrong-doer."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala aqoolu lakum AAindee khazainu Allahi wala aAAlamu alghayba wala aqoolu innee malakun wala aqoolu lillatheena tazdaree aAAyunukum lan yutiyahumu Allahu khayran Allahu aAAlamu bima fee anfusihim innee ithan lamina alththalimeena

32.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰنُوحُ قَدْ جَـٰدَلْتَنَا فَأَكْثَرْتَ جِدَٰلَنَا فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Noah, you have argued with us, and disputed at length; so bring that (retribution) you promise, if you speak the truth."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “O Nooh, you have disputed with us and disputed in the extreme, therefore bring upon us what you promise us, if you are truthful.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Noah, thou hast disputed with us and make much disputation with us. Then bring us that thou promisest us, if thou speakest truly.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: O Nuh! surely thou hast disputed with us and hath multiplied the disputation with us; now bring us that wherewith thou threatenest us, if thou be of the truth-tellers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "O Nuh (Noah)! You have disputed with us and much have you prolonged the dispute with us, now bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “O Noah, you have argued with us, and argued a great deal. Now bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They said: 'O Noah! Surely you have disputed with us and have prolonged your dispute. Now bring upon us the chastisement that you threaten us with; do so, if you are truthful.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "O Nuh! You have disputed with us, and much have you prolonged the dispute with us, now bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: O Noah! Thou hast disputed with us and multiplied disputation with us; now bring upon us that wherewith thou threatenest us, if thou art of the truthful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘O Noah, you have disputed with us already, and you have disputed with us exceedingly. Now bring us what you threaten us with, should you be truthful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They said: 'O Noah, you have disputed, and disputed too much, with us. Bring (down) upon us that which you promised us, if what you say is true'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "O Noah, you have disputed us and been frequent in dispute of us. So bring us what you threaten us, if you should be of the truthful."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "Noah, you have argued with us a great deal. Bring down on us whatever torment with which you have been threatening us if what you say is true".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: O Nuh! indeed you have disputed with us and lengthened dispute with us, therefore bring to us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Noah," they replied, "you have argued with us, and argued to excess. Now bring down upon us what you threaten us with, if you speak the truth!?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Noah! thou hast disputed with us, and (much) hast thou prolonged the dispute with us: now bring upon us what thou threatenest us with, if thou speakest the truth!?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ya noohu qad jadaltana faaktharta jidalana fatina bima taAAiduna in kunta mina alssadiqeena

33.arabic: قَالَ إِنَّمَا يَأْتِيكُم بِهِ ٱللَّهُ إِن شَآءَ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He replied: "Only God will bring it on you if He please, and you cannot prevail against Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “Allah will surely bring that upon you if He wills, and you will not be able to escape.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'God will bring you it if He will; you cannot frustrate Him.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: only Allah will bring it on you if He will, and ye shall not escape.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "Only Allah will bring it (the punishment) on you, if He will, and then you will escape not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “It is God who will bring it upon you, if He wills, and you will not be able to escape.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Noah said: 'Only Allah will bring it upon you if He so wills, and you will be utterly unable to frustrate that.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "Only Allah will bring it (the punishment) on you, if He wills, and then you will escape not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: Only Allah will bring it upon you if He will, and ye can by no means escape.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘Allah will indeed bring it on you if He wishes, and you cannot frustrate [Him].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He replied: 'Allah will bring it (down) upon you if He will; you will never frustrate Him.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "Allah will only bring it to you if He wills, and you will not cause [Him] failure.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Noah) replied, "God will bring torment down on you whenever He wants and you will not be able to make His (plans) fail.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: Allah only will bring it to you if He please, and you will not escape:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "It is God who will bring it down upon you, if He wishes, and you will not be able to escape.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "Truly, Allah will bring it on you if He wills,- and then, ye will not be able to frustrate it!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala innama yateekum bihi Allahu in shaa wama antum bimuAAjizeena

34.arabic: وَلَا يَنفَعُكُمْ نُصْحِىٓ إِنْ أَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَنصَحَ لَكُمْ إِن كَانَ ٱللَّهُ يُرِيدُ أَن يُغْوِيَكُمْ ۚ هُوَ رَبُّكُمْ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even if I wish to advise you aright, my counsel will not profit you if God intend that you go astray, for He is your Lord and to Him you will return."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And my advice will not benefit you if I wish you good, when Allah wills to keep you astray; He is your Lord and to Him you will return.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And my sincere counsel will not profit you, if I desire to counsel you sincerely, if God desires to pervert you; He is your Lord, and unto Him you shall be returned.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Nor would my good counsel profit you, even though I wished to give you good counsel, if Allah willed to keep you astray. He is your Lord, and unto Him ye shall be returned.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And my advice will not profit you, even if I wish to give you good counsel, if Allah's Will is to keep you astray. He is your Lord! and to Him you shall return."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “My advice will not benefit you, much as I may want to advise you, if God desires to confound you. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If I want to give you good advice that will not profit you if Allah Himself has decided to let you go astray. He is your Lord, and to Him will you be returned.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And my advice will not profit you, even if I wish to give you good counsel, if Allah's will is to keep you astray. He is your Lord! and to Him you shall return."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: My counsel will not profit you if I were minded to advise you, if Allah's will is to keep you astray. He is your Lord and unto Him ye will be brought back.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: My exhorting will not benefit you, much as I may seek to exhort you, if Allah desires to consign you to perversity. He is your Lord, and to Him you shall be brought back.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Nor will my sincere counsel benefit you if I sincerely desire to counsel you if Allah desires to lead you astray. He is your Lord, and to Him you shall return'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And my advice will not benefit you - although I wished to advise you - If Allah should intend to put you in error. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My advice will be of no benefit to you if God wants to let you go astray. He is your Lord and to Him you will all return."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if I intend to give you good advice, my advice will not profit you if Allah intended that He should leave you to go astray; He is your Lord, and to Him shall you be returned.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: My advice will not benefit you, no matter how sincerely I want to advise you, if God lets you go astray. He is your Lord and you will all return to Him."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Of no profit will be my counsel to you, much as I desire to give you (good) counsel, if it be that Allah willeth to leave you astray: He is your Lord! and to Him will ye return!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala yanfaAAukum nushee in aradtu an ansaha lakum in kana Allahu yureedu an yughwiyakum huwa rabbukum wailayhi turjaAAoona

35.arabic: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۖ قُلْ إِنِ ٱفْتَرَيْتُهُۥ فَعَلَىَّ إِجْرَامِى وَأَنَا۠ بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تُجْرِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do they say you have fabricated it? Tell them: "If I have fabricated it, then mine is the guilt; but I am clear of what you are guilty."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! They dare say that, “He has fabricated it”? Say, “If I may have fabricated it, then my sin is upon me, and I am unconcerned with your sins.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: (Or do they say, 'He has forged it'? Say: 'If I have forged it, upon me falls my sin; and I am quit of the sins you do.')

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or say they: he hath fabricated it? Say thou: on me then be my guilt, and I am quit of that whereof ye are guilty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or they (the pagans of Makkah) say: "He (Muhammad SAW) has fabricated it (the Quran)." Say: "If I have fabricated it, upon me be my crimes, but I am innocent of (all) those crimes which you commit."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they say, “He made it up?” Say, “If I made it up, upon me falls my crime, and I am innocent of the crimes you commit.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Muhammad!) Do they say that he himself has forged this message? Tell them: 'If I have forged this, the guilt of it will fall upon me, but I am not responsible for the crimes you are committing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or they (the pagans of Makkah) say: "He has fabricated it." Say: "If I have fabricated it, upon me be my crimes, but I am innocent of (all) those crimes which you commit.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or say they (again): He hath invented it? Say: If I have invented it, upon me be my crimes, but I am innocent of (all) that ye commit.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they say, ‘He has fabricated it?’ Say, ‘Should I have fabricated it, then my guilt will be upon me, and I am absolved of your guilty conduct.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or do they say: 'He has fabricated it (himself)' Say: 'If I had fabricated it, then the sin rests upon me. I reject the sins you do'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or do they say [about Prophet Muhammad], "He invented it"? Say, "If I have invented it, then upon me is [the consequence of] my crime; but I am innocent of what [crimes] you commit."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they say that Muhammad has falsely ascribed (the Quran) to God? (Muhammad), tell them "Had I falsely ascribed it to God, I shall be responsible for my own sins. I am certainly not responsible for whatever sins you commit!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or do they say: He has forged it? Say: If I have forged it, on me is my guilt, and I am clear of that of which you are guilty.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If they say, "He has invented it himself," say to them, "If I have indeed invented this myself, then may I be punished for my sin; I am innocent of the crimes that you commit."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or do they say, "He has forged it"? Say: "If I had forged it, on me were my sin! and I am free of the sins of which ye are guilty!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am yaqooloona iftarahu qul ini iftaraytuhu faAAalayya ijramee waana bareeon mimma tujrimoona

36.arabic: وَأُوحِىَ إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ أَنَّهُۥ لَن يُؤْمِنَ مِن قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَن قَدْ ءَامَنَ فَلَا تَبْتَئِسْ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And Noah was informed through revelation: "Apart from those who have come to believe already not one of your people is going to believe. So grieve not for what they are doing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And it was divinely revealed to Nooh that “None of your people will become Muslims, except those who have already accepted faith – therefore do not grieve at what they do.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And it was revealed to Noah, saying, 'None of thy people shall believe but he who has already believed; so be thou not distressed by that they may be doing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And it was revealed unto Nuh: verily none of thy people will believe save those that have believed already; so be not distressed for that which they have been doing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And it was inspired to Nuh (Noah): "None of your people will believe except those who have believed already. So be not sad because of what they used to do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And it was revealed to Noah: “None of your people will believe, except those who have already believed, so do not grieve over what they do.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: It was revealed to Noah that no more of your people, other than those who already believe, will ever come to believe. So do not grieve over their deeds,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And it was revealed to Nuh: "None of your people will believe except those who have believed already. So be not sad because of what they used to do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And it was inspired in Noah, (saying): No-one of thy folk will believe save him who hath believed already. Be not distressed because of what they do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It was revealed to Noah: ‘None of your people will believe except those who already have faith; so do not sorrow for what they used to do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And it was revealed to Noah: 'None of your nation will believe except whosoever has already believed. Do not distress yourself with what they may do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And it was revealed to Noah that, "No one will believe from your people except those who have already believed, so do not be distressed by what they have been doing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It was revealed to Noah that besides those who had already accepted his faith, no one from his people would ever believe him. He was told not to be disappointed about what his people had done,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And it was revealed to Nuh: That none of your people will believe except those who have already believed, therefore do not grieve at what they do:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God's will was revealed to Noah, "No more of your people will believe in you than those who already believe; do not grieve, therefore, over what they have been doing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It was revealed to Noah: "None of thy people will believe except those who have believed already! So grieve no longer over their (evil) deeds.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waoohiya ila noohin annahu lan yumina min qawmika illa man qad amana fala tabtais bima kanoo yafAAaloona

37.arabic: وَٱصْنَعِ ٱلْفُلْكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا وَوَحْيِنَا وَلَا تُخَـٰطِبْنِى فِى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُم مُّغْرَقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Build an ark under Our eye and as We instruct. Do not plead for those who have been wicked, for they shall certainly be drowned."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And build the ship in front of Us, and by Our command, and do not speak to Me regarding the unjust; they will surely be drowned.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Make thou the Ark under Our eyes, and as We reveal; and address Me not concerning those who have done evil; they shall be drowned.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And make thou the ark under Our eyes and Our Revelation; and address Me not regarding those that have done wrong; verily they are to be drowned.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And construct the ship under Our Eyes and with Our Inspiration, and address Me not on behalf of those who did wrong; they are surely to be drowned."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “And build the Ark, under Our eyes, and with Our inspiration, and do not address Me regarding those who did wrong; they are to be drowned.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and build the Ark under Our eyes and Our direction. And do not supplicate Me concerning those who have engaged in wrong-doing. They are doomed to be drowned.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And construct the ship under Our Eyes and with Our revelation, and address Me not on behalf of those who did wrong; they are surely to be drowned."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Build the ship under Our eyes and by Our inspiration, and speak not unto Me on behalf of those who do wrong. Lo! they will be drowned.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Build the ark before Our eyes and by Our revelation, and do not plead with Me for those who are wrongdoers: they shall indeed be drowned.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Build the Ark with Our seeing (and protection), and as We reveal. Do not speak to Me concerning the evildoers; they shall be drowned'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And construct the ship under Our observation and Our inspiration and do not address Me concerning those who have wronged; indeed, they are [to be] drowned."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: but to build the Ark under the supervision and guidance of his Lord. He was also told not to address any words to Him concerning the unjust for they were all to be drowned.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And make the ark before Our eyes and (according to) Our revelation, and do not speak to Me in respect of those who are unjust; surely they shall be drowned.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Build the Ark under Our eyes and in accordance with Our revelation. Do not plead with Me concerning the evil-doers. They shall certainly be drowned."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But construct an Ark under Our eyes and Our inspiration, and address Me no (further) on behalf of those who are in sin: for they are about to be overwhelmed (in the Flood)."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaisnaAAi alfulka biaAAyunina wawahyina wala tukhatibnee fee allatheena thalamoo innahum mughraqoona

38.arabic: وَيَصْنَعُ ٱلْفُلْكَ وَكُلَّمَا مَرَّ عَلَيْهِ مَلَأٌ مِّن قَوْمِهِۦ سَخِرُوا۟ مِنْهُ ۚ قَالَ إِن تَسْخَرُوا۟ مِنَّا فَإِنَّا نَسْخَرُ مِنكُمْ كَمَا تَسْخَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So he built the ark; and when groups of his people passed by him, they scoffed at him. He said to them: "Though you laugh at us (now), we shall laugh at you, as you are laughing at us.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And Nooh builds the ship; and whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they used to laugh at him; he said, “If you laugh at us, then a time will come when we will laugh at you just as you laugh.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So he was making the Ark; and whenever a council of his people passed by him they scoffed at him, He said, 'If you scoff at us, we shall surely scoff at you, as you scoff

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And he was making the ark; and whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they scoffed at him. He said: if ye scoff at us, verily we also scoff at you even as ye scoff at us.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And as he was constructing the ship, whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they made a mockery of him. He said: "If you mock at us, so do we mock at you likewise for your mocking.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As he was building the ark, whenever some of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said, “If you ridicule us, we will ridicule you, just as you ridicule.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As Noah was building the Ark, whenever the leading men of his nation passed by him, they would scoff at him. He said: 'If you scoff at us, we too scoff at you in like manner.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And as he was constructing the ship, whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they mocked at him. He said: "If you mock at us, so do we mock at you likewise for your mocking."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And he was building the ship, and every time that chieftains of his people passed him, they made mock of him. He said: Though ye make mock of Us, yet We mock at you even as ye mock;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As he was building the ark, whenever the elders of his people passed by him, they would ridicule him. He said, ‘If you ridicule us [today], we shall ridicule you [tomorrow] just as you ridicule us [now].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Whenever an assembly of his nation passed by him as he was building the Ark, they mocked him. Whereupon he said: 'If you mock us, we shall indeedmock you, just as you mock.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And he constructed the ship, and whenever an assembly of the eminent of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said, "If you ridicule us, then we will ridicule you just as you ridicule.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Noah) started to build the Ark but whenever some of his people passed by, they would mock him. He in return would reply, "Mock us, but just as you mock us, we, too, will mock you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And he began to make the ark; and whenever the chiefs from among his people passed by him they laughed at him. He said: If you laugh at us, surely we too laugh at you as you laugh (at us).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So he began to build the Ark, and whenever leaders of his people passed by, they scoffed at him. He said, "If you are scoffing at us, we shall scoff at you [and your ignorance], just as you scoff at us:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Forthwith he (starts) constructing the Ark: Every time that the chiefs of his people passed by him, they threw ridicule on him. He said: "If ye ridicule us now, we (in our turn) can look down on you with ridicule likewise!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WayasnaAAu alfulka wakullama marra AAalayhi malaon min qawmihi sakhiroo minhu qala in taskharoo minna fainna naskharu minkum kama taskharoona

39.arabic: فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَيَحِلُّ عَلَيْهِ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You will soon come to know who suffers the punishment that would put him to shame, and who suffers lasting torment."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “So now you will come to know – upon whom comes the punishment that disgraces him, and upon whom descends the punishment that continues forever.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and you shall know to whom will come a chastisement degrading him, and upon whom there shall alight a lasting chastisement.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So presently ye shall know on whom cometh a torment that humiliateth him, and on whom is let loose a torment lasting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And you will know who it is on whom will come a torment that will cover him with disgrace and on whom will fall a lasting torment."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “You will surely know upon whom will come a torment that will abase him, and upon whom will fall a lasting torment.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You will come to know who will be struck by a humiliating chastisement, and who will be subjected to an unceasing torment.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And you will know who it is on whom will come a torment that will cover him with disgrace and on whom will fall a lasting torment."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And ye shall know to whom a punishment that will confound him cometh, and upon whom a lasting doom will fall.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Soon you will know whom a disgraceful punishment will overtake and on whom a lasting punishment will descend.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You shall know to whom a degrading punishment will come, and upon whom an everlasting punishment will fall'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you are going to know who will get a punishment that will disgrace him [on earth] and upon whom will descend an enduring punishment [in the Hereafter]."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: You will soon learn who will face a humiliating punishment and will be encompassed by an everlasting torment."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So shall you know who it is on whom will come a chastisement which will disgrace him, and on whom will lasting chastisement come down.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: you will soon come to know who will receive a humiliating punishment, and find unleashed against him an everlasting punishment."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But soon will ye know who it is on whom will descend a penalty that will cover them with shame,- on whom will be unloosed a penalty lasting:"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fasawfa taAAlamoona man yateehi AAathabun yukhzeehi wayahillu AAalayhi AAathabun muqeemun

40.arabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا وَفَارَ ٱلتَّنُّورُ قُلْنَا ٱحْمِلْ فِيهَا مِن كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَن سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقَوْلُ وَمَنْ ءَامَنَ ۚ وَمَآ ءَامَنَ مَعَهُۥٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When Our command was issued and the waters gushed forth from the source, We said: "Take into (the ark) a pair of every species, and members of your family other than those against whom the sentence has been passed already, and those who come to believe." But only a few believed in him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: To the extent that when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, “Board into the ship a couple – male and female – from every kind, and your family members, except those upon whom the Word has been passed*, and all other Muslims”; and only a few Muslims were with him. (* Whose fate has been sealed.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Until, when Our command came, and the Oven boiled, We said, 'Embark in it two of every kind, and thy family -- except for him against whom the word has already been spoken and whosoever believes.' And there believed not with him except a few.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thus were they employed until when Our decree came and the oven boiled over, We said: carry thereon of every kind two, and thy household except him thereof against whom the word hath already gone forth, and whosoever hath believed. And there had not believed with him save a few.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (So it was) till then there came Our Command and the oven gushed forth (water like fountains from the earth). We said: "Embark therein, of each kind two (male and female), and your family, except him against whom the Word has already gone forth, and those who believe. And none believed with him, except a few."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Until, when Our command came, and the volcano erupted, We said, “Board into it a pair of every kind, and your family—except those against whom the sentence has already been passed—and those who have believed.” But those who believed with him were only a few.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus it was until Our command came to pass and the oven boiled over. We said: 'Take into the Ark a pair of every species; and take your own family except those who have already been declared (as unworthy); and also take everyone who believes. But those who, along with him, had believed were indeed just a few.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (So it was) till when Our command came and the oven gushed forth (water like fountains from the earth). We said: "Embark therein, of each kind two (male and female), and your family -- except him against whom the Word has already gone forth -- and those who believe. And none believed with him, except a few."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Thus it was) till, when Our commandment came to pass and the oven gushed forth water, We said: Load therein two of every kind, a pair (the male and female), and thy household, save him against whom the word hath gone forth already, and those who believe. And but a few were they who believed with him.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Our edict came and the oven gushed [a stream of water], We said, ‘Carry in it a pair of every kind [of animal], along with your family—except those [of them] against whom the edict has already been given—and those who have faith.’ And none believed with him except a few.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Our command came and the oven gushed (forth with water), We said (to Noah): 'Take on board (the Ark) a pair from every species and yourfamily, except he of whom the word has already been spoken, and those who believe. And none except a few believed with him.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [So it was], until when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, "Load upon the ship of each [creature] two mates and your family, except those about whom the word has preceded, and [include] whoever has believed." But none had believed with him, except a few.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When at last Our decree was fulfilled, water gushed forth from the Oven (in Noah's house). We told him to carry in the Ark a pair (male and female) from every species, his family - except those who were destined to perish - and the believers. No one believed in him, except a few.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Until when Our command came and water came forth from the valley, We said: Carry in it two of all things, a pair, and your own family-- except those against whom the word has already gone forth, and those who believe. And there believed not with him but a few.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Our command came, and water gushed forth in torrents, We said to Noah, "Take into the Ark a pair from every species, and your own family -- except those on whom the sentence has already been passed, and all the true believers." But only a few believed along with him.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: At length, behold! there came Our command, and the fountains of the earth gushed forth! We said: "Embark therein, of each kind two, male and female, and your family - except those against whom the word has already gone forth,- and the Believers." but only a few believed with him.

translations.author: Transliteration
