61.arabic: وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ هُوَ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيهَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى قَرِيبٌ مُّجِيبٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Be warned! Accursed are the people of 'Ad who were Hud's. To Thamud We Sent their brother Saleh. "O my people," he said, "worship God; you have no other god but He. It is He who raised you from the earth and settled you upon it. So beg your Lord to forgive you, and turn to Him in repentance. Surely my Lord is near and answers."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And to the Thamud tribe, We sent their fellow man Saleh; he said, “O my people! Worship Allah, there is no other True God except Him; He created you from the earth and established you in it, so seek forgiveness from Him and incline towards Him in repentance; indeed my Lord is Close, Acceptor of Prayer.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to Thamood their brother Salih; he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other than He. It is He who produced you from the earth and has given you to live therein; so ask forgiveness of Him, then repent to Him; surely my Lord is nigh, and answers prayer.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And unto Thamud We sent their brother Saleh. He said: O my people! worship Allah; there is no god for you but he. He hath caused you to spring out of the earth, and hath made you dwell therein. Wherefore ask forgiveness of Him, then repent toward Him; verily my Lord is Nigh, Responsive.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to Thamud (people, We sent) their brother Salih (Saleh). He said: "O my people! Worship Allah, you have no other Ilah (God) but Him. He brought you forth from the earth and settled you therein, then ask forgiveness of Him and turn to Him in repentance. Certainly, my Lord is Near (to all by His Knowledge), Responsive."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And to Thamood, their brother Saleh. He said, “O my people, worship God, you have no god other than Him. He initiated you from the earth, and settled you in it. So seek His forgiveness, and repent to Him. My Lord is Near and Responsive.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And to Thamud We sent their brother Sali'h. He said: 'My people! Serve Allah; you have no god other than Him. He brought you into being out of the earth, and has made you dwell in it. So ask Him to forgive you, and do turn towards Him in repentance. Indeed My Lord is near, responsive to prayers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to Thamud (people We sent) their brother Salih. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah: you have no other god but Him. He brought you forth from the earth and settled you therein, then ask forgiveness of Him and turn to Him in repentance. Certainly, my Lord is Near (to all by His knowledge), Responsive."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And unto (the tribe of) Thamud (We sent) their brother Salih. He said: O my people! Serve Allah, Ye have no other Allah save Him. He brought you forth from the earth and hath made you husband it. So ask forgiveness of Him and turn unto Him repentant. Lo! my Lord is Nigh, Responsive.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And to Thamud [We sent] Salih, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other god besides Him. He brought you forth from the earth and made it your habitation. So plead with Him for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. My Lord is indeed nearmost [and] responsive.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And to Thamood, (We sent) their brother Salih. He said: 'My nation, worship Allah; you have no god except He. It is He who made you from the earth and let you live upon it. Ask His forgiveness then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is near and answers (prayers)'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to Thamud [We sent] their brother Salih. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. He has produced you from the earth and settled you in it, so ask forgiveness of Him and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To Thamud We sent their brother Salih who told them, "My people, worship God; He is your only Lord. It is He who has created you from the earth and has settled you therein. Seek forgiveness from Him and turn to Him in repentance. My Lord is certainly close to everyone and He hears all prayers."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to Samood (We sent) their brother Salih. He said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than He; He brought you into being from the earth, and made you dwell in it, therefore ask forgiveness of Him, then turn to Him; surely my Lord is Nigh, Answering.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To the Thamud We sent their brother Salih. He said, "My people, worship God! You have no god but Him. It was He who brought you into being from the earth and settled you upon it and so ask His forgiveness. Turn to Him in repentance. My Lord is near and responsive."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To the Thamud People (We sent) Salih, one of their own brethren. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah: ye have no other god but Him. It is He Who hath produced you from the earth and settled you therein: then ask forgiveness of Him, and turn to Him (in repentance): for my Lord is (always) near, ready to answer."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waila thamooda akhahum salihan qala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu huwa anshaakum mina alardi waistaAAmarakum feeha faistaghfiroohu thumma tooboo ilayhi inna rabbee qareebun mujeebun

62.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰصَـٰلِحُ قَدْ كُنتَ فِينَا مَرْجُوًّا قَبْلَ هَـٰذَآ ۖ أَتَنْهَىٰنَآ أَن نَّعْبُدَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَا وَإِنَّنَا لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَآ إِلَيْهِ مُرِيبٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Saleh, we had placed our hopes in you, but you forbid us from worshipping that which our fathers worshipped, and we are suspicious of what you are calling us to."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “O Saleh! You seemed very promising among us, before this – What! You forbid us from worshipping the deities of our forefathers? And indeed we have fallen into a deeply intriguing doubt concerning what you call us to.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Salih, thou hast hitherto been a source of hope among us. What, dost thou forbid us to serve that our fathers served? Truly we are in doubt, concerning what thou callest us to, disquieting.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: O Saleh! heretofore thou wast amongst us as one hoped for. Forbiddest us thou to worship that which our fathers have worshipped? And verily we are regarding that to which thou callest us in doubt disquieting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "O Salih (Saleh)! You have been among us as a figure of good hope (and we wished for you to be our chief), till this [new thing which you have brought; that we leave our gods and worship your God (Allah) Alone]! Do you (now) forbid us the worship of what our fathers have worshipped? But we are really in grave doubt as to that which you invite us to (monotheism)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “O Saleh, we had hopes in you before this. Are you trying to prevent us from worshiping what our parents worship? We are in serious doubt regarding what you are calling us to.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They said: 'O Salih! Until now you were one of those among us on whom we placed great hopes. Now, would you forbid us to worship what our forefathers were wont to worship? Indeed we are in disquieting doubt about what you are calling us to.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "O Salih! You have been among us as a figure of good hope until this! Do you (now) forbid us the worship of what our fathers have worshipped But we are really in grave doubt as to that to which you invite us."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: O Salih! Thou hast been among us hitherto as that wherein our hope was placed. Dost thou ask us not to worship what our fathers worshipped? Lo! we verily are in grave doubt concerning that to which thou callest us.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘O Salih! You were a source of hope to us before this. Do you forbid us to worship what our fathers have been worshiping? Indeed we have grave doubts concerning that to which you invite us.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They said: 'Salih, before this you were a source of hope to us. What, would you now forbid us to worship what our fathers worshipped? Indeed, we are disquieted, and doubt that to which you invite'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "O Salih, you were among us a man of promise before this. Do you forbid us to worship what our fathers worshipped? And indeed we are, about that to which you invite us, in disquieting doubt."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "Salih, we had great hope in you before this. Do you forbid us to worship that which our fathers had worshipped? We are doubtful and uncertain about what you have told us to worship."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: O Salih! surely you were one amongst us in whom great expectations were placed before this; do you (now) forbid us from worshipping what our fathers worshipped? And as to that which you call us to, most surely we are in disquieting doubt.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "O Salih! We had great hopes in you. Do you forbid us to worship what our fathers worshipped? We are in grave doubt, amounting to suspicion, concerning that to which you call us."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Salih! thou hast been of us! a centre of our hopes hitherto! dost thou (now) forbid us the worship of what our fathers worshipped? But we are really in suspicious (disquieting) doubt as to that to which thou invitest us."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ya salihu qad kunta feena marjuwwan qabla hatha atanhana an naAAbuda ma yaAAbudu abaona wainnana lafee shakkin mimma tadAAoona ilayhi mureebun

63.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَءَاتَىٰنِى مِنْهُ رَحْمَةً فَمَن يَنصُرُنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِنْ عَصَيْتُهُۥ ۖ فَمَا تَزِيدُونَنِى غَيْرَ تَخْسِيرٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people, have you considered that if my Lord has clearly shown me the way, and I have His blessings too, who will save me then from God if I disobey? You will only add to my ruin.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O my people! What is your opinion – if I am on clear proof from my Lord and He has bestowed upon me mercy from Him, so who will rescue me from Allah if I disobey Him? So you will not increase anything for me, except loss!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'O my people, what think you? If I stand upon a clear sign from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Him, who shall help me against God if I rebel against Him? You would do nothing for me, except increase my loss.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said; O my people! bethink if I rest on an evidence from my Lord, and there hath come to me from Him a mercy, then who will succour me against Allah, if I disobey Him? Ye then increase me not save in loss.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear proof from my Lord, and there has come to me a Mercy (Prophethood, etc.) from Him, who then can help me against Allah, if I were to disobey Him? Then you increase me not but in loss.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O my people, have you considered? If I stand upon clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Him, who would protect me from God, if I disobeyed Him? You add nothing for me except loss.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Salih said: 'My people! What do you think? If I had a clear evidence from my Lord, and then He also bestowed His mercy upon me, who will rescue me from the punishment of Allah if I still disobey Him? You can only make me lose even more.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear proof from my Lord, and there has come to me a mercy from Him, who then can help me against Allah, if I were to disobey Him Then you increase me not but in loss."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O my people! Bethink you: if I am (acting) on clear proof from my Lord and there hath come unto me a mercy from Him, who will save me from Allah if I disobey Him? Ye would add to me naught save perdition.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O my people! Tell me, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and He has granted me His own mercy, who will protect me from Allah should I disobey Him? For then you will increase me in nothing but loss.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He said: 'My nation, think! If I have a clear proof from my Lord and He has given me His Mercy, who would help me against Allah if I rebel against Him? Indeed, you would do nothing for me except increase my loss'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "O my people, have you considered: if I should be upon clear evidence from my Lord and He has given me mercy from Himself, who would protect me from Allah if I disobeyed Him? So you would not increase me except in loss.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He said, "My people, think. I have received authoritative evidence and mercy from my Lord, so who will protect me from God if I disobey Him? You certainly want to destroy me.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O my people! tell me if I have clear proof from my Lord and He has granted to me mercy from Himself-- who will then help me against Allah if I disobey Him? Therefore you do not add to me other than loss:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "O my people, tell me: if I have clear evidence from my Lord, and He has granted His mercy, who then will support me against God, if I disobey Him? You would only make my loss greater.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people! do ye see? if I have a Clear (Sign) from my Lord and He hath sent Mercy unto me from Himself,- who then can help me against Allah if I were to disobey Him? What then would ye add to my (portion) but perdition?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya qawmi araaytum in kuntu AAala bayyinatin min rabbee waatanee minhu rahmatan faman yansurunee mina Allahi in AAasaytuhu fama tazeedoonanee ghayra takhseerin

64.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةً فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ قَرِيبٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O my people, this she-camel of God is a token for you. So leave her alone to graze on God's earth, and do not molest her, otherwise the swiftest punishment would befall you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And O my people! This is the she-camel of Allah – a sign for you – so let her graze in Allah’s earth, and do not touch her with an evil intention for an imminent punishment will reach you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O my people, this is the She-camel of God, to be a sign for you. Leave her that she may eat in God's earth, and touch her not with evil, lest you' be seized by a nigh chastisement.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And O my people! yonder is the she-camel of Allah: a sign unto you; so let her alone, feeding in Allah's land, and touch her not with evil, lest there may overtake you a torment nigh.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign to you, leave her to feed on Allah's earth, and touch her not with evil, lest a near torment will seize you."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O my people, this is the she-camel of God, a sign for you. Let her graze on God’s land, and do not harm her, lest an imminent punishment overtakes you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. So let her pasture on Allah's earth, and do not hurt her or else some chastisement - which is near at hand -should overtake you.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign to you, so leave her to graze in Allah's land, and touch her not with evil, lest a near torment should seize you."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O my people! This is the camel of Allah, a token unto you, so suffer her to feed in Allah's earth, and touch her not with harm lest a near torment seize you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. Let her graze [freely] in Allah’s land, and do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by a prompt punishment.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'My nation, this is the shecamel of Allah, a sign for you. Leave her to graze upon the earth of Allah and do not touch her with evil lest you be seized by a punishment that is near'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And O my people, this is the she-camel of Allah - [she is] to you a sign. So let her feed upon Allah 's earth and do not touch her with harm, or you will be taken by an impending punishment."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My people, this is the she-camel of God, a miracle (to support the truth which has been brought) to you. Leave her to graze in the land of God. Do not harm her with your evil deeds lest torment will suddenly strike you."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And, O my people! this will be (as) Allah's she-camel for you, a sign; therefore leave her to pasture on Allah's earth and do not touch her with evil, for then a near chastisement will overtake you.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: My people! This she-camel of God is a sign for you. So leave her alone to graze on God's earth. And do her no harm, lest you should be instantly afflicted with a torment."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a symbol to you: leave her to feed on Allah's (free) earth, and inflict no harm on her, or a swift penalty will seize you!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waya qawmi hathihi naqatu Allahi lakum ayatan fatharooha takul fee ardi Allahi wala tamassooha bisooin fayakhuthakum AAathabun qareebun

65.arabic: فَعَقَرُوهَا فَقَالَ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ فِى دَارِكُمْ ثَلَـٰثَةَ أَيَّامٍ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ وَعْدٌ غَيْرُ مَكْذُوبٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But they hamstrung her. Then (Saleh) said: "You have but three days to enjoy life in your homes. Infallible is this promise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In response they hamstrung her, he therefore said, “Enjoy a further three days in your homes; this is a promise that will not be untrue.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But they hamstrung her; and he said, 'Take your joy in your habitation three days -- that is a promise not to be belied.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Yet they hamstrang her. Then he said: enjoy yourselves in your dwellings three days: that is a promise, not to be falsified.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But they killed her. So he said: "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. This is a promise (i.e. a threat) that will not be belied."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But they hamstrung her, and so He said, “Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. This is a prophecy that is infallible.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But they slaughtered her. Thereupon Salih warned them: 'Enjoy yourselves in your homes for a maximum of three days. This is a promise which shall not be belied.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But they slaughtered her. So he said: "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. This is a promise (a threat) that will not be belied."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But they hamstrung her, and then he said: Enjoy life in your dwelling-place three days! This is a threat that will not be belied.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But they hamstrung her, whereupon he said, ‘Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days: that is a promise not to be belied!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Yet they hamstrung her. He (Salih) said: 'Take your enjoyment in your homes for three days! This is a promise that cannot be belied'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But they hamstrung her, so he said, "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. That is a promise not to be denied."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When they slew the she-camel, Salih told them, "You have only three days to enjoy living in your homes (before you will be struck by the torment). This is an inevitable prophecy."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But they slew her, so he said: Enjoy yourselves in your abode for three days, that is a promise not to be belied.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But they hamstrung her. He [Salih] said, "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three more days. This warning will not prove false."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But they did ham-string her. So he said: "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days: (Then will be your ruin): (Behold) there a promise not to be belied!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FaAAaqarooha faqala tamattaAAoo fee darikum thalathata ayyamin thalika waAAdun ghayru makthoobin

66.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا صَـٰلِحًا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَمِنْ خِزْىِ يَوْمِئِذٍ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ ٱلْقَوِىُّ ٱلْعَزِيزُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, when Our command (of punishment) came We delivered Saleh by Our grace, and those who believed, with him, from the disgrace of that day. Verily your Lord is mighty and powerful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore when Our command came, We rescued Saleh and the Muslims who were with him by Our mercy, and from the disgrace of that day; indeed your Lord is the Strong, the Almighty.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Our command came, We delivered Salih and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us, and from the degradation of that day; thy Lord is the All-strong, the All-mighty.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then when Our decree came to pass We delivered Saleh and those who believed with Him by a mercy from Us, and from the humiliation of that day. Verily thy Lord! He is the Strong, the Mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So when Our Commandment came, We saved Salih (Saleh) and those who believed with him by a Mercy from Us, and from the disgrace of that Day. Verily, your Lord, He is the All-Strong, the All-Mighty.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then, when Our command came, We saved Saleh and those who believed with him, by a mercy from Us, from the disgrace of that day. Your Lord is the Strong, the Mighty.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then, when Our command came to pass, We saved Salih and those who shared his faith through Our special mercy, from the disgrace of that day. Truly Your Lord is All-Strong, All-Mighty.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So when Our commandment came, We saved Salih and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us, and from the disgrace of that Day. Verily, your Lord -- He is the All-Strong, the All-Mighty.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So, when Our commandment came to pass, We saved Salih, and those who believed with him, by a mercy from Us, from the ignominy of that day. Lo, thy Lord! He is the Strong, the Mighty.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So when Our edict came, We delivered Salih and the faithful who were with him by a mercy from Us and from the [punishment and] disgrace of that day. Your Lord is indeed the All-strong, the All-mighty.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Our command came, We saved Salih together with those who believed with him through Our Mercy, and from the ignominy of that day. YourLord is Strong and Mighty.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So when Our command came, We saved Salih and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and [saved them] from the disgrace of that day. Indeed, it is your Lord who is the Powerful, the Exalted in Might.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Our decree came to pass. We mercifully saved Salih and his faithful followers from that day's ignominy. Your Lord is certainly Mighty and Majestic.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So when Our decree came to pass, We delivered Salih and those who believed with him by mercy from Us, and (We saved them) from the disgrace of that day; surely your Lord is the Strong, the Mighty.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then when Our command came by Our grace, We saved Salih and those who believed along with him from the disgrace of that day. Surely, your Lord is powerful and mighty.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When Our Decree issued, We saved Salih and those who believed with him, by (special) Grace from Ourselves - and from the Ignominy of that day. For thy Lord - He is the Strong One, and able to enforce His Will.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falamma jaa amruna najjayna salihan waallatheena amanoo maAAahu birahmatin minna wamin khizyi yawmiithin inna rabbaka huwa alqawiyyu alAAazeezu

67.arabic: وَأَخَذَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَـٰرِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The sinners were seized by a blast from heaven, and lay overturned in their homes in the morning,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the terrible scream seized the unjust – so at morning they remained lying flattened in their homes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And the evildoers were seized by the Cry, and morning found them in their habitations fallen prostrate

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the shout overtook those who had done wrong; so they lay in their dwellings crouching

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And As-Saihah (torment - awful cry, etc.) overtook the wrong-doers, so they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the Scream struck those who transgressed, and they became motionless bodies in their homes.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And the Blast overtook those who were wont to do wrong, and then they lay lifeless in their homes

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And As-Sayhah (awful cry) overtook the wrongdoers, so they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And the (awful) Cry overtook those who did wrong, so that morning found them prostrate in their dwellings,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The Cry seized those who were wrongdoers, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And the harmdoers were seized by the Shout (of the angel), and in the morning, they were found crouched in their dwellings, dead,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A blast struck the unjust and they were found lying motionless on their faces

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the rumbling overtook those who were unjust, so they became motionless bodies in their abodes,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The wrongdoers were overtaken by a dreadful blast and they lay dead in their homes,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The (mighty) Blast overtook the wrong-doers, and they lay prostrate in their homes before the morning,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waakhatha allatheena thalamoo alssayhatu faasbahoo fee diyarihim jathimeena

68.arabic: كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ثَمُودَا۟ كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمْ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّثَمُودَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As though they had never lived there at all. Beware! The people of Thamud turned away from their Lord. Beware! Accursed are the people of Thamud.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: As if they had never lived there; indeed the tribe of Thamud disbelieved in their Lord; away with the Thamud!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: as if they never dwelt there: 'Surely Thamood disbelieved in their Lord, so away with Thamood!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: As though they had never lived at ease therein. Lo! verily Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. Lo! away with Thamud.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As if they had never lived there. No doubt! Verily, Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. So away with Thamud!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As if they had never prospered therein. Indeed, Thamood rejected their Lord, so away with Thamood.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: as though they had never lived there before. Oh, verily the Thamud denied their Lord! Oh, the Thamud were destroyed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As if they had never lived there. No doubt! Verily, Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. So away with Thamud!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As though they had not dwelt there. Lo! Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. A far removal for Thamud!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: as if they had never lived there. Behold! Thamud indeed defied their Lord. Now, away with Thamud!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: as though they had never lived there. Indeed, Thamood disbelieved in their Lord. So, begone Thamood.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As if they had never prospered therein. Unquestionably, Thamud denied their Lord; then, away with Thamud.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: as though they had never existed. The people of Thamud denied the existence of their Lord. How distant from the mercy of God had the people of Thamud gone!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: As though they had never dwelt in them; now surely did Samood disbelieve in their Lord; now surely, away with Samood.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: as if they had never dwelt there. The Thamud denied their Lord; cursed are the tribe of Thamud.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: As if they had never dwelt and flourished there. Ah! Behold! for the Thamud rejected their Lord and Cherisher! Ah! Behold! removed (from sight) were the Thamud!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kaan lam yaghnaw feeha ala inna thamooda kafaroo rabbahum ala buAAdan lithamooda

69.arabic: وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ بِٱلْبُشْرَىٰ قَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمًا ۖ قَالَ سَلَـٰمٌ ۖ فَمَا لَبِثَ أَن جَآءَ بِعِجْلٍ حَنِيذٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Our angels came to Abraham with good news, and said: "Peace on you. "Peace on you too," said Abraham, and hastened to bring a roasted calf.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed Our angels came to Ibrahim with glad tidings – they said, “Peace”; he answered, “Peace” and without delay brought a roasted calf.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings; they said, 'Peace!' 'Peace,' he said; and presently he brought a roasted calf.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly Our messengers came unto Ibrahim with the glad tidings. The said: peace! He said: peace! And he tarried not till he brougt a calf roasted.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And verily, there came Our Messengers to Ibrahim (Abraham) with glad tidings. They said: Salam (greetings or peace!) He answered, Salam (greetings or peace!) and he hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news. They said, “Peace.” He said, “Peace.” Soon after, he came with a roasted calf.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed Our messengers came to Abraham, bearing glad tidings. They greeted him with 'peace', and Abraham answered back to them 'peace', and hurriedly brought to them a roasted calf.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And verily, there came Our messengers to Ibrahim with the glad tidings. They said: "Salaman (greetings or peace!)" He answered, "Salamun (greetings or peace!)," and he hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Our messengers cam unto Abraham with good news. They said: Peace! He answered: Peace! and delayed not to bring a roasted calf.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news, and said, ‘Peace!’ ‘Peace!’ He replied. Presently he brought [for them] a roasted calf.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Our Messengers came to Abraham with glad tidings. They said: 'Peace' He replied 'Peace' and in a while brought them a roasted calf.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And certainly did Our messengers come to Abraham with good tidings; they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and did not delay in bringing [them] a roasted calf.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Our Messengers came to Abraham with glad news. They said, "Peace be with you." He replied similarly. After a short time he presented them with a roasted calf

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly Our apostles came to Ibrahim with good news. They said: Peace. Peace, said he, and he made no delay in bringing a roasted calf.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news and greeted him with, "Peace." He too said, "Peace be on you," and hastened to bring a roasted calf.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: There came Our messengers to Abraham with glad tidings. They said, "Peace!" He answered, "Peace!" and hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad jaat rusuluna ibraheema bialbushra qaloo salaman qala salamun fama labitha an jaa biAAijlin haneethin

70.arabic: فَلَمَّا رَءَآ أَيْدِيَهُمْ لَا تَصِلُ إِلَيْهِ نَكِرَهُمْ وَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً ۚ قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِ لُوطٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When they did not stretch their hands towards it he became suspicious and afraid of them. They said: "Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he thought they were pretending and inwardly started fearing them; they said, “Do not be afraid – we are sent to the people of Lut.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he was suspicious of them and conceived a fear of them. They said, 'Fear not; we have been sent to the people of Lot.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when he beheld that their hands reached it not he misliked them, and ccnceived a fear of them. They said fear not verily we are sent unto the people of Lut.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But when he saw their hands went not towards it (the meal), he felt some mistrust of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not, we have been sent against the people of Lout (Lot)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he became suspicious of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said, “Do not fear, we were sent to the people of Lot.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When he perceived that their hands could not reach it, he mistrusted them, and felt afraid of them. They said: 'Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But when he saw their hands went not towards it (the meal), he mistrusted them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not, we have been sent against the people of Lut."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when he saw their hands reached not to it, he mistrusted them and conceived a fear of them. They said: Fear not! Lo! we are sent unto the folk of Lot.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he took them amiss and felt a fear of them. They said, ‘Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But when he saw that their hands did not reach towards it, he was wary and became fearful of them, but they said: 'Do not be afraid. We are sent to thenation of Lot'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he distrusted them and felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not. We have been sent to the people of Lot."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: but when he saw that their hands did not reach out for it, he could not know who they were and became afraid of them. They said, "Do not be afraid; we are God's angelic Messengers sent to the people of Lot."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But when he saw that their hands were not extended towards it, he deemed them strange and conceived fear of them. They said: Fear not, surely we are sent to Lut's people.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But when he saw that they made no move to eat, he found this strange and became afraid of them. They said, "Do not be afraid, for we have been sent to the people of Lot."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But when he saw their hands went not towards the (meal), he felt some mistrust of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not: We have been sent against the people of Lut."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falamma raa aydiyahum la tasilu ilayhi nakirahum waawjasa minhum kheefatan qaloo la takhaf inna orsilna ila qawmi lootin

71.arabic: وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ قَآئِمَةٌ فَضَحِكَتْ فَبَشَّرْنَـٰهَا بِإِسْحَـٰقَ وَمِن وَرَآءِ إِسْحَـٰقَ يَعْقُوبَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: His wife who stood near, laughed as We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac of Jacob.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And his wife was standing by and she started laughing*, so We gave her glad tidings regarding Ishaq, and following Ishaq, regarding Yaqub.** (* She was glad that the disbelieving people of Lut would be destroyed.** The birth of these two.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And his wife was standing by; she laughed, therefore We gave her the glad tidings of Isaac, and, after Isaac, of Jacob.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And his wife was standing. she laughed. Then We gave her the glad tidings of Is'haq, and after Is'haq, Ya'qub.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed (either, because the Messengers did not eat their food or for being glad for the destruction of the people of Lout (Lot). But We gave her glad tidings of Ishaque (Isaac), and after him, of Ya'qub (Jacob).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: His wife was standing by, so she laughed. And We gave her good news of Isaac; and after Isaac, Jacob.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And Abraham's wife was standing by and on hearing this she laughed. And We gave her the good news of (the birth of) Isaac, and after Isaac, of Jacob.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed. But We gave her glad tidings of Ishaq, and after Ishaq, of Ya`qub.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And his wife, standing by laughed when We gave her good tidings (of the birth) of Isaac, and, after Isaac, of Jacob.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: His wife, standing by, laughed as We gave her the good news of [the birth of] Isaac, and of Jacob, after Isaac.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: His wife (Sarah), who was standing nearby, laughed. Thereupon, We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac of Jacob.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And his Wife was standing, and she smiled. Then We gave her good tidings of Isaac and after Isaac, Jacob.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: His wife who was standing nearby, smiled and so We gave her the glad news that she would give birth to Isaac who would have a son, Jacob.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And his wife was standing (by), so she laughed, then We gave her the good news of Ishaq and after Ishaq of (a son's son) Yaqoub.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: His wife, who was standing nearby, laughed when We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac, Jacob.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed: But we gave her glad tidings of Isaac, and after him, of Jacob.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waimraatuhu qaimatun fadahikat fabashsharnaha biishaqa wamin warai ishaqa yaAAqooba

72.arabic: قَالَتْ يَـٰوَيْلَتَىٰٓ ءَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا۠ عَجُوزٌ وَهَـٰذَا بَعْلِى شَيْخًا ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَىْءٌ عَجِيبٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: She said: "Woe betide me! Will I give birth when I am old and this my husband be aged? This is indeed surprising!"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: She said, “Oh woe to me – will I bear a child whereas I am an old woman, and this my husband, is an old man? This is something really extraordinary.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: She said, 'Woe is me! Shall I bear, being an old woman, and this my husband is an old man? This assuredly is a strange thing.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: She said: Oh for me shall bring forth when I am old, and this my husband is advanced in years! Verily a marvellous thing is this!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: She said (in astonishment): "Woe unto me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman, and here is my husband, an old man? Verily! This is a strange thing!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: She said, “Alas for me. Shall I give birth, when I am an old woman, and this, my husband, is an old man? This is truly a strange thing.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: She said: 'Woe is me! Shall I bear a child now that I am an old woman and my husband is well advanced in years. This is indeed strange!'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: She said (in astonishment): "Woe unto me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman, and here is my husband an old man Verily, this is a strange thing!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: She said: Oh woe is me! Shall I bear a child when I am an old woman, and this my husband is an old man? Lo! this is a strange thing!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: She said, ‘Oh, my! Shall I, an old woman, bear [children], and [while] this husband of mine is an old man?! That is indeed an odd thing!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: She replied: 'Alas for me! Shall I bear (a child) when I am old woman and my husband is advanced in years? This is indeed a strange thing'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: She said, "Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man? Indeed, this is an amazing thing!"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: She said, "Woe is me! How can I have a baby when I am barren and my husband is very old? This is certainly strange".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: She said: O wonder! shall I bear a son when I am an extremely old woman and this my husband an extremely old man? Most surely this is a wonderful thing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: She said, "Alas! Shall I bear a child in this old age, while my husband here is also old? This is indeed a strange thing!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: She said: "Alas for me! shall I bear a child, seeing I am an old woman, and my husband here is an old man? That would indeed be a wonderful thing!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qalat ya waylata aalidu waana AAajoozun wahatha baAAlee shaykhan inna hatha lashayon AAajeebun

73.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَعْجَبِينَ مِنْ أَمْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ رَحْمَتُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَـٰتُهُۥ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ ٱلْبَيْتِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَمِيدٌ مَّجِيدٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Why are you surprised at the command of God? God's mercy and blessings be upon you, O members of this household," they said. "Verily He is worthy of praise and glory."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The angels said, “Do you wonder at the command of Allah? Allah’s mercy and His blessings be upon you, O people of this house; indeed He only is Most Praiseworthy, Most Honourable.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'What, dost thou marvel at God's command? The mercy of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of the House! Surely He is All-laudable, All-glorious.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said marvellest thou at the decree of Allah? Mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house, verily He is Praiseworthy, Glorious.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "Do you wonder at the Decree of Allah? The Mercy of Allah and His Blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibrahim (Abraham)]. Surely, He (Allah) is All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “Do you marvel at the decree of God? The mercy and blessings of God are upon you, O people of the house. He is Praiseworthy and Glorious.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They said: 'Do you wonder at Allah's decree? Allah's mercy and His blessings be upon you, O people of the house. Surely, He is Praiseworthy, Glorious.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "Do you wonder at the decree of Allah The mercy of Allah and His blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibrahim]. Surely, He (Allah) is All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: Wonderest thou at the commandment of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, O people of the house! Lo! He is Owner of Praise, Owner of Glory!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘Are you amazed at Allah’s dispensation? [That is] Allah’s mercy and His blessings upon you, members of the household. Indeed He is all-laudable, all-glorious.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They said: 'What, do you marvel at the command of Allah? The Mercy of Allah and His Blessings be upon you, O people of the House. Indeed, He isPraised, Exalted'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy and Honorable."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They replied, "Would you be surprised at God's decree? People of the house, may God's mercy and blessings be with you. God is Appreciative and Glorious."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: Do you wonder at Allah's bidding? The mercy of Allah and His blessings are on you, O people of the house, surely He is Praised, Glorious.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Are you astonished at God's command? May the mercy of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of this house. Surely, He is praiseworthy and glorious."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "Dost thou wonder at Allah's decree? The grace of Allah and His blessings on you, o ye people of the house! for He is indeed worthy of all praise, full of all glory!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ataAAjabeena min amri Allahi rahmatu Allahi wabarakatuhu AAalaykum ahla albayti innahu hameedun majeedun

74.arabic: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنْ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلرَّوْعُ وَجَآءَتْهُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ يُجَـٰدِلُنَا فِى قَوْمِ لُوطٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When Abraham's fear was dispelled, and the good news had come to him, he pleaded for the people of Lot with Us.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Ibrahim’s fear abated and the glad tidings reached him, he argued with Us regarding the people of Lut.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So, when the awe departed from Abraham and the good tidings came to him, he was disputing with Us concerning the people of Lot;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then when the alarm had departed from Ibrahim and the glad tidings had come home unto him, he took to disputing with us for the people of Lut

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then when the fear had gone away from (the mind of) Ibrahim (Abraham), and the glad tidings had reached him, he began to plead with Us (Our Messengers) for the people of Lout (Lot).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When Abraham's fear subsided, and the good news had reached him, he started pleading with Us concerning the people of Lot.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus when fear had left Abraham and the good news had been conveyed to him, he began to dispute with Us concerning the people of Lot.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then when the fear had gone away from (the mind of) Ibrahim, and the glad tidings had reached him, he began to plead with Us (Our messengers) for the people of Lut.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when the awe departed from Abraham, and the glad news reached him, he pleaded with Us on behalf of the folk of Lot.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So when the awe had left Abraham and the good news had reached him, he pleaded with Us concerning the people of Lot.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when the wonderment departed from Abraham and the glad tidings reached him, he pleaded with Us for the nation of Lot;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when the fright had left Abraham and the good tidings had reached him, he began to argue with Us concerning the people of Lot.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Abraham had controlled his fear and received the glad news, he started to plead with Us for the people of Lot;

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So when fear had gone away from Ibrahim and good news came to him, he began to plead with Us for Lut's people.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When the fear had left Abraham, and the glad tidings had been conveyed to him, he began to plead with Us for Lot's people,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When fear had passed from (the mind of) Abraham and the glad tidings had reached him, he began to plead with us for Lut's people.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falamma thahaba AAan ibraheema alrrawAAu wajaathu albushra yujadiluna fee qawmi lootin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Abraham was kind, compassionate, and penitent.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed Ibrahim is most forbearing, very soft hearted, penitent.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Abraham was clement, compassionate, penitent.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily Ibrahim was forbearing, long-suffering, penitent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, Ibrahim (Abraham) was, without doubt, forbearing, used to invoke Allah with humility, and was repentant (to Allah all the time, again and again).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Abraham was gentle, kind, penitent.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely Abraham was forbearing, tenderhearted and oft-turning to Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, Ibrahim was, without doubt, forbearing, used to invoke Allah with humility, and was repentant.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! Abraham was mild, imploring, penitent.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Abraham was indeed most forbearing, plaintive, [and] penitent.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: indeed, Abraham was forbearing, tenderhearted and penitent.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, Abraham was forbearing, grieving and [frequently] returning [to Allah].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Abraham was certainly a forbearing, compassionate, and tender-hearted person.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely Ibrahim was forbearing, tender-hearted, oft-returning (to Allah):

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: for Abraham was forbearing, tender-hearted and oft-returning to God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For Abraham was, without doubt, forbearing (of faults), compassionate, and given to look to Allah.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna ibraheema lahaleemun awwahun muneebun

76.arabic: يَـٰٓإِبْرَٰهِيمُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هَـٰذَآ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ قَدْ جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَإِنَّهُمْ ءَاتِيهِمْ عَذَابٌ غَيْرُ مَرْدُودٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Desist from pleading, O Abraham," (they said). "Your Lord's command has verily been issued, and a punishment that cannot be averted is bound to fall on them."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “O Ibrahim, turn away from this; indeed your Lord’s command has come; and indeed a punishment will come upon them, which will not be averted.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'O Abraham, turn away from this; thy Lord's command has surely come, and there is coming upon them a chastisement not to be turned back.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ibrahim! leave off this; verily the decree from thy Lord hath already come, and verily they! upon them is coming a torment unavoidable.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "O Ibrahim (Abraham)! Forsake this. Indeed, the Commandment of your Lord has gone forth. Verily, there will come a torment for them which cannot be turned back."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O Abraham, refrain from this. The command of your Lord has come; they have incurred an irreversible punishment.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thereupon (Our angels) said to him: 'O Abraham! Desist from this, for indeed your Lord's command has come; and a chastisement which cannot be averted is about to befall them.' words literally suggest; the purpose is merely to express a sense of wonder.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "O Ibrahim! Forsake this. Indeed, the commandment of your Lord has gone forth. Verily, there will come a torment for them which cannot be turned back."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (It was said) O Abraham! Forsake this! Lo! thy Lord's commandment hath gone forth, and lo! there cometh unto them a doom which cannot be repelled.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘O Abraham, let this matter alone! Your Lord’s edict has certainly come, and an irrevocable punishment shall overtake them.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (It was said:) 'Abraham, turn away from this; your Lord's command has indeed come, and there is coming upon them a punishment that will not be turned back'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [The angels said], "O Abraham, give up this [plea]. Indeed, the command of your Lord has come, and indeed, there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We said, "Abraham, avoid asking Us such questions. Your Lord's decree has already been issued and an inevitable torment will strike these people."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O Ibrahim! leave off this, surely the decree of your Lord has come to pass, and surely there must come to them a chastisement that cannot be averted.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We said, "Abraham, cease your pleading: the command of your Lord has gone forth. There shall fall upon them a punishment which none can avert!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O Abraham! Seek not this. The decree of thy Lord hath gone forth: for them there cometh a penalty that cannot be turned back!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ibraheemu aAArid AAan hatha innahu qad jaa amru rabbika wainnahum ateehim AAathabun ghayru mardoodin

77.arabic: وَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًا سِىٓءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًا وَقَالَ هَـٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَصِيبٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So when Our angels came to Lot, he grieved for them, and felt powerless to help them, and said: "This is a day of sorrow.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Our angels came to Lut, he was distressed for them and was disheartened due to them, and said, “This is a day of great hardship.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was troubled on their account and distressed for them, and he said, 'This is a fierce day.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when Our messengers came unto Lut, he was distressed on their account, and he felt straitened on their account, and he said: this is a lay dreadful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when Our Messengers came to Lout (Lot), he was grieved on their account and felt himself straitened for them (lest the town people should approach them to commit sodomy with them). He said: "This is a distressful day."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when Our envoys came to Lot, he was anxious for them, and concerned for them. He said, “This is a dreadful day.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was perturbed by their coming and felt troubled on their account, and said: 'This is a distressing day.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when Our messengers came to Lut, he was grieved on account of them and was concerned for them. He said: "This is a distressful day."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Our messengers came unto Lot, he was distressed and knew not how to protect them. He said: This is a distressful day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account and in a predicament for their sake, and he said, ‘This is a terrible day!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was troubled and distressed for them, and said: 'This is a harsh day'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when Our messengers, [the angels], came to Lot, he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said, "This is a trying day."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Our Messengers came to Lot, he became sorrowful and felt totally helpless. He said, "This is indeed a distressful day".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Our apostles came to Lut, he was grieved for them, and he lacked strength to protect them, and said: This is a hard day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was uneasy on this account and felt powerless to protect them. He said, "This is a dreadful day."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When Our messengers came to Lut, he was grieved on their account and felt himself powerless (to protect) them. He said: "This is a distressful day."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walamma jaat rusuluna lootan seea bihim wadaqa bihim tharAAan waqala hatha yawmun AAaseebun

78.arabic: وَجَآءَهُۥ قَوْمُهُۥ يُهْرَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ وَمِن قَبْلُ كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ بَنَاتِى هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخْزُونِ فِى ضَيْفِىٓ ۖ أَلَيْسَ مِنكُمْ رَجُلٌ رَّشِيدٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: His people came excited to him. They were addicted to sin already. Said (Lot): "O my people, these daughters of mine are cleaner (and lawful) for you. Have fear of God, and do not shame me before my guests. Is there no man of discernment among you?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And his people came running towards him; and they were in the habit of committing evil deeds; he said, “O my people! These women of the tribe are my daughters* – they are purer for you – therefore fear Allah and do not disgrace me in the midst of my guests; is there not even a single righteous man among you?” (* The wives of those people.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And his people came to him, running towards him; and erstwhile they had been doing evil deeds. He said, 'O my people, these are my daughters; they are cleaner for you. So fear God, and do not degrade me in my guests. What, is there not one man among you of a right mind?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And his people came unto him rushing on toward him, and afore they were wont to work vices. He said. O my people! these are my daughters: purer are they for you; so fear Allah, and humiliate me not in the face of my guests; is there not among you any man right-minded?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And his people came rushing towards him, and since aforetime they used to commit crimes (sodomy, etc.), he said: "O my people! Here are my daughters (i.e. the daughters of my nation), they are purer for you (if you marry them lawfully). So fear Allah and degrade me not as regards my guests! Is there not among you a single right-minded man?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And his people came rushing towards him—they were in the habit of committing sins. He said, “O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear God, and do not embarrass me before my guests. Is there not one reasonable man among you?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And his people came to him rushing. Before this they were wont to commit evil deeds. Lot said: 'My people! Here are my daughters; they are purer for you. Have fear of Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not even one right-minded person in your midst?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And his people came rushing towards him, and since aforetime they used to commit crimes, he said: "O my people! Here are my daughters, they are purer for you. So have Taqwa of Allah and disgrace me not with regard to my guests! Is there not among you a single right-minded man"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And his people came unto him, running towards him - and before then they used to commit abominations - He said: O my people! Here are my daughters! They are purer for you. Beware of Allah, and degrade me not in (the person of) my guests. Is there not among you any upright man?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then his people came running toward him, and they had been committing vices aforetime. He said, ‘O my people, these are my daughters: they are purer for you. Be wary of Allah and do not humiliate me with regard to my guests. Is there not a right-minded man among you?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: His nation came running towards him; whilst they were doing evil deeds. 'My nation' he said: 'here are my daughters (take them in marriage), they are cleaner for you. Fear Allah and do not humiliate me by my guests. Is there not one man amongst you of right mind'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And his people came hastening to him, and before [this] they had been doing evil deeds. He said, "O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of reason?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: His people, who had constantly indulged in evil deeds, came running to him. He said, "My people, here are my pure daughters. Have fear of God and do not humiliate me before my guests. Is there no person of understanding among you?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And his people came to him, (as if) rushed on towards him, and already they did evil deeds. He said: O my people! these are my daughters-- they are purer for you, so guard against (the punishment of) Allah and do not disgrace me with regard to my guests; is there not among you one right-minded man?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: His people, who were used to committing foul deeds, came running to him. He said, "My people, here are my daughters. They are purer for you, [if you marry] so have some fear of God and do not disgrace me before my guests. Is there not a single right-minded man among you?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And his people came rushing towards him, and they had been long in the habit of practising abominations. He said: "O my people! Here are my daughters: they are purer for you (if ye marry)! Now fear Allah, and cover me not with shame about my guests! Is there not among you a single right-minded man?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wajaahu qawmuhu yuhraAAoona ilayhi wamin qablu kanoo yaAAmaloona alssayyiati qala ya qawmi haolai banatee hunna atharu lakum faittaqoo Allaha wala tukhzooni fee dayfee alaysa minkum rajulun rasheedun

79.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا لَنَا فِى بَنَاتِكَ مِنْ حَقٍّ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعْلَمُ مَا نُرِيدُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said: "You know we have no need for your daughters, and know well what we want."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “You know that we have no right to the daughters of your tribe; and you obviously know what we desire.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Thou knowest we have no right to thy daughters, and thou well knowest what we desire.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: assuredly thou knowest that we have no right to thy daughters, and verily thou knowest that which we would have.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "Surely you know that we have neither any desire nor in need of your daughters, and indeed you know well what we want!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “You know well that we have no right to your daughters, and you know well what we want.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They said: 'Surely you already know that we have nothing to do with your daughters. You also know well what we want.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "Surely, you know that we have no need of your daughters, and indeed you know well what we want!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: Well thou knowest that we have no right to thy daughters, and well thou knowest what we want.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘You certainly know that we have no interest in your daughters, and indeed you know what we want.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They replied: 'You know we have no right for your daughters. You know very well what we desire'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "You have already known that we have not concerning your daughters any claim, and indeed, you know what we want."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "You certainly know that we have no right to your daughters and you know what we want".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: Certainly you know that we have no claim on your daughters, and most surely you know what we desire.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They replied, "You know we have no need of your daughters. You know very well what we are seeking."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "Well dost thou know we have no need of thy daughters: indeed thou knowest quite well what we want!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo laqad AAalimta ma lana fee banatika min haqqin wainnaka lataAAlamu ma nureedu

80.arabic: قَالَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِى بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ ءَاوِىٓ إِلَىٰ رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "I wish I had the power to resist you," said (Lot), "or powerful support."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “If only I had the strength against you or were able to get the refuge of some strong support!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'O would that I had power against you, or might take refuge in a strong pillar!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said. Would that I had strength against you or could betake me to a powerful support!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "Would that I had strength (men) to overpower you, or that I could betake myself to some powerful support (to resist you)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “If only I had the strength to stop you, or could rely on some strong support.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He said: 'Would that I had the strength to set you straight, or could seek refuge in some powerful support.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "Would that I had strength to overpower you, or that I could betake myself to some powerful support."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: Would that I had strength to resist you or had some strong support (among you)!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘If only I had the power to deter you, or could take refuge in a mighty support!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He said: 'Would that I were powerful over you, or might take refuge in a strong pillar'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "If only I had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He said, "Would that I had the power (to overcome you) or could seek strong protection."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: Ah! that I had power to suppress you, rather I shall have recourse to a strong support.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "If only I had the strength to stop you or could take refuge in some powerful support!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "Would that I had power to suppress you or that I could betake myself to some powerful support."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala law anna lee bikum quwwatan aw awee ila ruknin shadeedin

81.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰلُوطُ إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَن يَصِلُوٓا۟ إِلَيْكَ ۖ فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ وَلَا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنكُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَكَ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ مُصِيبُهَا مَآ أَصَابَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ ٱلصُّبْحُ ۚ أَلَيْسَ ٱلصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (The angels) said: "O Lot, we have verily been sent by your Lord. They will never be able to harm you. So, leave late at night with your family, and none of you should turn back to look; but your wife will suffer (the fate) they are going to suffer. Their hour of doom is in the morning: Is not the morning nigh?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The angels said, “O Lut! We are the sent ones of your Lord – they cannot get to you, therefore during the night take your entire household with you – and not one of you may turn around and see – except your wife*; she too will be afflicted with the same as they will be; indeed their promise is at morn; is not the morning imminent?” (* She was a disbeliever and sided with the culprits.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Lot, we are messengers of thy Lord. They shall not reach thee; so set forth, thou with thy family, in a watch of the night, and let not any one of you turn round, excepting thy wife; surely she shall be smitten by that which smites them. Their promised time is the morning; is the morning not nigh?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: O Lut! verily we are messengers of thy Lord; they shall by no means reach thee: go forth thou With thy household in a part of the night, and let none of you look back, save thy wife; verily that which befalleth them shall befall her; verily their appointment is for the morning; is not morning nigh?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They (Messengers) said: "O Lout (Lot)! Verily, we are the Messengers from your Lord! They shall not reach you! So travel with your family in a part of the night, and let not any of you look back, but your wife (will remain behind), verily, the punishment which will afflict them, will afflict her. Indeed, morning is their appointed time. Is not the morning near?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “O Lot, we are the envoys of your Lord; they will not reach you. So set out with your family during the cover of the night, and let none of you look back, except for your wife. She will be struck by what will strike them. Their appointed time is the morning. Is not the morning near?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thereupon the angels said: 'O Lot! We indeed are messengers of your Lord. And your people will in no way be able to hurt you. So depart with your family in a part of the night and let no one of you turn around excepting your wife (who shall not go); for what will befall them shall also befall her. In the morning their promised hour will come. Is not the morning near?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They (messengers) said: "O Lut! Verily, we are the messengers from your Lord! They shall not reach you! So travel with your family in a part of the night, and let not any of you look back; but your wife (will remain behind), verily, the punishment which will afflict them, will afflict her. Indeed, morning is their appointed time. Is not the morning near"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (The messengers) said: O Lot! Lo! we are messengers of thy Lord; they shall not reach thee. So travel with thy people in a part of the night, and let not one of you turn round - (all) save thy wife. Lo! that which smiteth them will smite her (also). Lo! their tryst is (for) the morning. Is not the morning nigh?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘O Lot, we are messengers of your Lord. They will never get at you. Set out with your family in a watch of the night; and none of you shall turn round, except your wife; indeed she will be struck by what strikes them. Indeed their tryst is the dawn. Is not the dawn [already] near?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They (the angels) said: 'Lot, we are the Messengers of your Lord, they shall not touch you. Depart with your family in the watch of the night and let none of you turn round, except your wife. She shall be struck by that which strikes them. Their appointed time is the morning. Is it not that the morning is near'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The angels said, "O Lot, indeed we are messengers of your Lord; [therefore], they will never reach you. So set out with your family during a portion of the night and let not any among you look back - except your wife; indeed, she will be struck by that which strikes them. Indeed, their appointment is [for] the morning. Is not the morning near?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Our Messengers said, "Lot, we are the Messengers of your Lord. They will never harm you. Leave the town with your family in the darkness of night and do not let any of you turn back. As for your wife, she will suffer what they (unbelievers) will suffer. Their appointed time will come at dawn. Surely dawn is not far away!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: O Lut! we are the apostles of your Lord; they shall by no means reach you; so remove your followers in a part of the night-- and let none of you turn back-- except your wife, for surely whatsoever befalls them shall befall her; surely their appointed time is the morning; is not the morning nigh?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Lot, we are your Lord's messengers. By no means shall they reach you! So depart with your family while it is yet night and let none of you look back. But your wife will suffer the fate that befalls the others. Their appointed time is the morning: is the morning not near?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (The Messengers) said: "O Lut! We are Messengers from thy Lord!
