By no means shall they reach thee! now travel with thy family while yet a part of the night remains, and let not any of you look back: but thy wife (will remain behind): To her will happen what happens to the people. Morning is their time appointed: Is not the morning nigh?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ya lootu inna rusulu rabbika lan yasiloo ilayka faasri biahlika biqitAAin mina allayli wala yaltafit minkum ahadun illa imraataka innahu museebuha ma asabahum inna mawAAidahumu alssubhu alaysa alssubhu biqareebin

82.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا جَعَلْنَا عَـٰلِيَهَا سَافِلَهَا وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا حِجَارَةً مِّن سِجِّيلٍ مَّنضُودٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So when the decreed moment arrived, We turned the habitations upside down, and rained upon them stones of hardened lava in quick succession,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So when Our command came, We turned that township upside down and showered it continuously with stones of fired clay.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So when Our command came, We turned it uppermost nethermost, and rained on it stones of baked clay, one on another,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then when Our decree came to pass, We turned the upside thereof downward, and We rained thereon stones of baked clay, piled up.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So when Our Commandment came, We turned (the towns of Sodom in Palestine) upside down, and rained on them stones of baked clay, piled up;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when Our command came about, We turned it upside down, and We rained down on it stones of baked clay.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And when Our command came to pass, We turned the town upside down, and rained on it stones of baked clay, one on another,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So when Our commandment came, We turned them upside down, and rained on them stones of clay, in an array.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So when Our commandment came to pass We overthrew (that township) and rained upon it stones of clay, one after another,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So when Our edict came, We made its topmost part its nethermost, and We rained on it stones of laminar shale,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Our command came, We turned it upside down, and rained on it, stone after stone of baked clay,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So when Our command came, We made the highest part [of the city] its lowest and rained upon them stones of layered hard clay, [which were]

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Our decree came to pass, We turned the town upside-down and showered unto it lumps of baked clay,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So when Our decree came to pass, We turned them upside down and rained down upon them stones, of what had been decreed, one after another.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Our command came, We turned that town upside down and We rained upon it stones of clay, layer upon layer,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When Our Decree issued, We turned (the cities) upside down, and rained down on them brimstones hard as baked clay, spread, layer on layer,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falamma jaa amruna jaAAalna AAaliyaha safilaha waamtarna AAalayha hijaratan min sijjeelin mandoodin

83.arabic: مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا هِىَ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بِبَعِيدٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Impressed with (the signs) of your Lord. And such (punishment) is not far for the (other) transgressers.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: That are marked, in the custody your Lord; and those stones are not at all far from the unjust!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: marked with thy Lord, and never far from the evildoers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Marked from before thy Lord. Nor are they from the wrong- doers far away.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Marked from your Lord, and they are not ever far from the Zalimun (polytheists, evil-doers, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Marked from your Lord, and never far from the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: marked from your Lord. Nor is the punishment far off from the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Marked from your Lord; and they are not ever far from the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Marked with fire in the providence of thy Lord (for the destruction of the wicked). And they are never far from the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: sent in from your Lord [for the profligate], never far from the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: marked with your Lord, and never far from the evildoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Marked from your Lord. And Allah 's punishment is not from the wrongdoers [very] far.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: marked by your Lord. Such a punishment is not far away from the unjust people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Marked (for punishment) with your Lord and it is not far off from the unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: marked for them by the decree of your Lord. The punishment of the unjust was not far off.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Marked as from thy Lord: Nor are they ever far from those who do wrong!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Musawwamatan AAinda rabbika wama hiya mina alththalimeena bibaAAeedin

84.arabic: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ وَلَا تَنقُصُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ ۚ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُم بِخَيْرٍ وَإِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ مُّحِيطٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We sent to Midian their brother Shu'aib. He said: "O my people, worship God; you have no other god but He. So do not give in short measure nor underweigh. I see you are prosperous, but I fear the doom of an overwhelming Day for you.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And to Madyan their fellow man Shuaib; he said, “O my people! Worship Allah – there is no other True God except Him; and do not make reductions in measure and weight – indeed I see you prosperous, and I fear the punishment of the Besieging Day (of Resurrection) upon you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to Midian their brother Shuaib; he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other than He. And diminish not the measure and the balance. I see you are prospering; and I fear for you the chastisement of an encompassing day.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And unto Madyan We sent their brother Shu'aib. He said: O my people! worship Allah; there is no god for you but he. And give not short measure and weight. Verily I see you in prosperity, and verily I fear for you the torment of a Day encompassing

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to the Madyan (Midian) people (We sent) their brother Shu'aib. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah, you have no other Ilah (God) but Him, and give not short measure or weight, I see you in prosperity; and verily I fear for you the torment of a Day encompassing.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And to Median, their brother Shuaib. He said, “O my people, worship God; you have no god other than Him. And do not short measure or short weight. I see you in good circumstances, but I fear for you the agony of an encompassing Day.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And to (the people of) Midian We sent their brother Shu'ayb. He said: 'My people! Serve Allah; you have no god other than Him. And do not diminish the measure and weight. Indeed I see that you are prospering now, but I fear for you the chastisement of an encompassing day in the future.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to the Madyan people (We sent) their brother Shu`ayb. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah, you have no other god but Him, and give not short measure or weight. I see you in prosperity and verily, I fear for you the torment of a Day encompassing."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And unto Midian (We sent) their brother Shu'eyb. He said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him! And give not short measure and short weight. Lo! I see you well-to-do, and lo! I fear for you the doom of a besetting Day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And to Midian [We sent] Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other god besides Him. Do not diminish the measure or the balance. Indeed I see that you are faring well, but I fear for you the punishment of an all-embracing day.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And to Midian, (We sent) their brother Shu'aib. He said: 'My nation, worship Allah; you have no god except He. Do not reduce the measure nor the scale. I see you are prosperous, and fear the punishment of an encompassing day for you.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to Madyan [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. And do not decrease from the measure and the scale. Indeed, I see you in prosperity, but indeed, I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing Day.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To the people of Midian We sent their brother Shu'ayb who told them, "My people, worship God; He is your only Lord. Do not be dishonest in your weighing and measuring. I can see you are safe and prosperous, but I am afraid for you of the overwhelming torment of the (appointed) day.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to Madyan (We sent) their brother Shu'aib. He said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than He, and do not give short measure and weight: surely I see you in prosperity and surely I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To the people of Midian, We sent their brother Shu'ayb. He said, "My people, worship God. You have no deity other than Him. Do not give short measure and short weight. I see you are prospering. I fear for you the punishment of a fateful Day.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To the Madyan People (We sent) Shu'aib, one of their own brethren: he said: "O my people! worship Allah: Ye have no other god but Him. And give not short measure or weight: I see you in prosperity, but I fear for you the penalty of a day that will compass (you) all round.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waila madyana akhahum shuAAayban qala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu wala tanqusoo almikyala waalmeezana innee arakum bikhayrin wainnee akhafu AAalaykum AAathaba yawmin muheetin

85.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, O my people, weigh and measure with justness, and do not withhold things due to men, and do not spread corruption in the land, despoiling it.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “O my people! Measure and weigh in full with justice, and do not give the people their goods diminished, and do not roam about in the earth causing turmoil.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O my people, fill up the measure and the balance justly, and do not diminish the goods of the people, and do not mischief in the land, working corruption.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And, O my people! give full measure and weight with equity, and defraud not the people of their things, and commit not mischief on the earth as corrupters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And O my people! Give full measure and weight in justice and reduce not the things that are due to the people, and do not commit mischief in the land, causing corruption.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O my people! Give full measure and full weight, in all fairness, and do not cheat the people out of their rights, and do not spread corruption in the land.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My people! Give full measure and weight with justice, do not diminish the goods of others, and do not go about creating corruption in the land.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And O my people! Give full measure and weight in justice and reduce not the things that are due to the people, and do not commit mischief in the land, causing corruption."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O my people! Give full measure and full weight in justice, and wrong not people in respect of their goods. And do not evil in the earth, causing corruption.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘O my people! Observe fully the measure and the balance, with justice, and do not cheat the people of their goods, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'My nation, be just in filling the measure and the scale. Do not reduce people's goods and do not corrupt the land with mischief.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And O my people, give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive the people of their due and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My people, be just in your weighing and measuring. Do not defraud people or spread evil in the land.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And, O my people! give full measure and weight fairly, and defraud not men their things, and do not act corruptly in the land, making mischief:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: O my people, [always] give full measure and weight, in all fairness and do not defraud people by making short delivery, and do not spread corruption in the land.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And O my people! give just measure and weight, nor withhold from the people the things that are their due: commit not evil in the land with intent to do mischief.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waya qawmi awfoo almikyala waalmeezana bialqisti wala tabkhasoo alnnasa ashyaahum wala taAAthaw fee alardi mufsideena

86.arabic: بَقِيَّتُ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ ۚ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ عَلَيْكُم بِحَفِيظٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That which is left to you by God is better, if you are true believers; yet I am not a warden over you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “That which remains from Allah’s bestowal is better for you, if you believe; and I am not at all a guardian over you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God's remainder is better for you, if you are believers. And I am not a guardian over you.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the remainder of Allah is better for you, if ye believers, and I am not over you a guardian.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "That which is left by Allah for you (after giving the rights of the people) is better for you, if you are believers. And I am not set over you as a guardian.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: What is left by God is best for you, if you are believers. And I am not a guardian over you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The gains that Allah lets you retain are better for you, if you indeed believe. In any case, I have not been appointed a keeper over you.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "That which is left by Allah is better for you, if you are believers. And I am not a guardian over you."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That which Allah leaveth with you is better for you if ye are believers; and I am not a keeper over you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘What remains of Allah’s provision is better for you, should you be faithful, and I am not a keeper over you.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: What remains with Allah is better for you, if you are believers. I am not your guardian'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: What remains [lawful] from Allah is best for you, if you would be believers. But I am not a guardian over you."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If you are true believers then know that the profit which God has left for you is better for you (than what you may gain through deceitful ways). I am not responsible for your deeds."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What remains with Allah is better for you if you are believers, and I am not a keeper over you.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: What God leaves with you is the best for you, if you are believers. I have not been appointed as your keeper."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "That which is left you by Allah is best for you, if ye (but) believed! but I am not set over you to keep watch!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Baqiyyatu Allahi khayrun lakum in kuntum mumineena wama ana AAalaykum bihafeethin

87.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ أَصَلَوٰتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَن نَّتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَآ أَوْ أَن نَّفْعَلَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِنَا مَا نَشَـٰٓؤُا۟ ۖ إِنَّكَ لَأَنتَ ٱلْحَلِيمُ ٱلرَّشِيدُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Shu'aib, does your piety teach that we should abandon what our fathers worshipped, or desist from doing what we like with our goods? How gracious a man of discernment you are indeed!"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “O Shuaib! Does your prayer command you that we forsake the deities of our forefathers or that we may not do as we wish with our own property? Yes indeed – only you are very intelligent, most righteous*.” (* They mocked at him with sarcasm.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Shuaib, does thy prayer command thee that we should leave that our fathers served, or to do as we will with our goods? Thou art the clement one, the right-minded.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: Shu'aib, commandeth thee thy prayer that we should abandon that which our fathers have worshipped, or that we should not do with our riches whatsoever we will! thou, indeed! thou forsooth art forbearing, right-minded!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "O Shu'aib! Does your Salat (prayer) (i.e. the prayers which you offer has spoiled your mind, so you) command that we leave off what our fathers used to worship, or that we leave off doing what we like with our property? Verily, you are the forbearer, right-minded!" (They said this sarcastically).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “O Shuaib, does your prayer command you that we abandon what our ancestors worshiped, or doing with our wealth what we want? You are the one who is intelligent and wise.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They replied: 'O Shu'ayb! Does your Prayer enjoin upon you that we should forsake the deities whom our forefathers worshipped, or that we should give up using our wealth as we please? Do you fancy that you, and only you, are forbearing and right-directed?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "O Shu`ayb! Does your Salah command you that we give up what our fathers used to worship, or that we give up doing what we like with our property Verily, you are the forbearer, right-minded!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: O Shu'eyb! Doth thy way of prayer command thee that we should forsake that which our fathers (used to) worship, or that we (should leave off) doing what we will with our own property. Lo! thou art the mild, the guide to right behaviour.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘O Shu‘ayb, does your worship require that we abandon what our fathers have been worshiping, or that we should not do with our wealth whatever we wish? You are indeed [a] gentle and sensible [person].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Shu'aib' they replied, 'does your prayer command you that we should abandon that which our fathers worshipped or do as we please with our goods? You are clement and rightminded'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "O Shu'ayb, does your prayer command you that we should leave what our fathers worship or not do with our wealth what we please? Indeed, you are the forbearing, the discerning!"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They asked him, "Shu'ayb, do your prayers tell you that we must give up the worship of what our fathers had worshipped and that we must not deal with our properties as we like? We still believe that you are a person of forbearance and understanding."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: O Shu'aib! does your prayer enjoin you that we should forsake what our fathers worshipped or that we should not do what we please with regard to our property? Forsooth you are the forbearing, the right-directing one.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Shu'ayb, does your prayer tell you that we should abandon what our forefathers worshipped and that we should stop disposing of our belongings as we please? You are indeed the only wise and rightly-guided man!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Shu'aib! Does thy (religion of) prayer command thee that we leave off the worship which our fathers practised, or that we leave off doing what we like with our property? truly, thou art the one that forbeareth with faults and is right-minded!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ya shuAAaybu asalatuka tamuruka an natruka ma yaAAbudu abaona aw an nafAAala fee amwalina ma nashao innaka laanta alhaleemu alrrasheedu

88.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَرَزَقَنِى مِنْهُ رِزْقًا حَسَنًا ۚ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُخَالِفَكُمْ إِلَىٰ مَآ أَنْهَىٰكُمْ عَنْهُ ۚ إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا ٱلْإِصْلَـٰحَ مَا ٱسْتَطَعْتُ ۚ وَمَا تَوْفِيقِىٓ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ ۚ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people, think. I have a clear sign from my Lord, who has also given me a goodly provision, and I do not wish for myself what I forbid you: I only wish to reform you as best I can. My success is from God alone. In Him I have placed my trust, and to Him I turn.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O my people! What is your opinion – if I am on a clear proof from my Lord and He has bestowed me with an excellent sustenance from Him (shall I ignore all this?); and the matter I forbid you to do, I do not wish that I myself act against it; I only intend to make improvements as far possible; my guidance is only from Allah; I rely only upon Him and towards Him only do I incline.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'O my people, what think you? If I stand upon a clear sign from my Lord, and He has provided me with fair provision from Him -- and I desire not to come behind you, betaking me to that I forbid you; I desire only to set things right, so far as I am able. My succour is only with God; in Him I have put my trust, and to Him I turn, penitent.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: O my people! bethink if I rested on an evidence from my Lord, and He hath provided me with a goodly provision from Himself, shell I fail to deliver His message! And I desire not, in order to oppose you, to do that which I forbid I desire not but rectification, so far you as I am able, and my hope of success is not save with Allah; in Him I rely and unto Him I turn penitently.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me a good sustenance from Himself (shall I corrupt it by mixing it with the unlawfully earned money). I wish not, in contradiction to you, to do that which I forbid you. I only desire reform so far as I am able, to the best of my power. And my guidance cannot come except from Allah, in Him I trust and unto Him I repent.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O my people, have you considered? What if I have clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me good livelihood from Himself? I have no desire to do what I forbid you from doing. I desire nothing but reform, as far as I can. My success lies only with God. In Him I trust, and to Him I turn.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Shu'ayb said: 'My people! What do you think? If I stand on clear evidence from my Lord, and He has also provided me a handsome provision from Himself -(should I be ungrateful to Him and share your error and iniquity?) Nor do I desire to act contrary to what I admonish you. I desire nothing but to set things right as far as I can. My succour is only with Allah. In Him have I put my trust, and to Him do I always turn.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me if I have a clear evidence from my Lord and He has given me a good sustenance from Himself (shall I corrupt it by mixing it with the unlawfully earned money). I wish not, in contradiction to you, to do that which I forbid you. I only desire reform to the best of my power. And my guidance cannot come except from Allah, in Him I put my trust and unto Him I repent."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O my people! Bethink you: if I am (acting) on a clear proof from my Lord and He sustaineth me with fair sustenance from Him (how can I concede aught to you)? I desire not to do behind your backs that which I ask you not to do. I desire naught save reform so far as I am able. My welfare is only in Allah. In Him I trust and unto Him I turn (repentant).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O my people! Have you considered, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, who has provided me a good provision from Himself? I do not wish to oppose you by what I forbid you. I only desire to put things in order, as far as I can, and my success lies only with Allah: in Him I have put my trust, and to Him I turn penitently.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He said: 'Think, my nation! If I have a clear sign from my Lord and He has provided me with good provision, I do not desire to go behind you, taking for myself that which I forbid you. I seek but to reform as much as I can, my help comes only from Allah. In Him I have put my trust and to Him I turn in penitence.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "O my people, have you considered: if I am upon clear evidence from my Lord and He has provided me with a good provision from Him...? And I do not intend to differ from you in that which I have forbidden you; I only intend reform as much as I am able. And my success is not but through Allah. Upon him I have relied, and to Him I return.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He said, "My people, do you not realize that I have received authoritative evidence from my Lord and have been granted a noble gift from Him? I do not want to oppose or ignore what I have prohibited you not to do. I only intend to reform you as much as I can. My success is in the hands of God. I trust Him and turn to Him in repentance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O my people! have you considered if I have a clear proof from my Lord and He has given me a goodly sustenance from Himself, and I do not desire that in opposition to you I should betake myself to that which I forbid you: I desire nothing but reform so far as I am able, and with none but Allah is the direction of my affair to a right issue; on Him do I rely and to Him do I turn:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: [Shu'ayb] said, "O my people! What do you think? If I have clear evidence from my Lord, and He has sustained me with fair sustenance from Himself [should I not guide you?]. I have no desire to do, out of opposition to you, what I am asking you not to do. I only want to reform you as far as I can. Nor can I succeed without God's help. In Him I have put my trust and to Him I turn.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people! see ye whether I have a Clear (Sign) from my Lord, and He hath given me sustenance (pure and) good as from Himself? I wish not, in opposition to you, to do that which I forbid you to do. I only desire (your) betterment to the best of my power; and my success (in my task) can only come from Allah. In Him I trust, and unto Him I look.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya qawmi araaytum in kuntu AAala bayyinatin min rabbee warazaqanee minhu rizqan hasanan wama oreedu an okhalifakum ila ma anhakum AAanhu in oreedu illa alislaha ma istataAAtu wama tawfeeqee illa biAllahi AAalayhi tawakkaltu wailayhi oneebu

89.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ لَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شِقَاقِىٓ أَن يُصِيبَكُم مِّثْلُ مَآ أَصَابَ قَوْمَ نُوحٍ أَوْ قَوْمَ هُودٍ أَوْ قَوْمَ صَـٰلِحٍ ۚ وَمَا قَوْمُ لُوطٍ مِّنكُم بِبَعِيدٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "O my people (I fear) lest your opposition to me should bring you the like of what befell the people of Noah or Hud or Saleh; and the people of Lot are not distant from you.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And O my people! May not your opposition to me occasion the coming upon you of the thing similar to what befell the people of Nooh or the people of Hud or the people of Saleh; and the people of Lut are not at all far from you!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O my people, let not the breach with me move you, so that there smite you the like of what smote the people of Noah, or the people of Hood, or the people of Salih; and the people of Lot are not far away from you.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And, O my people! let not the cleavage with me incite you so that there befall you the like of that which befell the people of Nuh and the people of Hud and the people of Saleh; and the people of Lut are not from you far away.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And O my people! Let not my Shiqaqcause you to suffer the fate similar to that of the people of Nuh (Noah) or of Hud or of Salih (Saleh), and the people of Lout (Lot) are not far off from you!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O my people, let not your hostility towards me cause you to suffer what was suffered by the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Saleh. The people of Lot are not far away from you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My people! Let not your opposition to me lead you to guilt that would bring upon you the chastisement that struck earlier the people of Noah, and the people of Hud, and the people of Salih. And the land of the people of Lot is not far from you!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And O my people! Let not my Shiqaq cause you to suffer the fate similar to that of the people of Nuh or of Hud or of Salih, and the people of Lut are not far off from you!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And, O my people! Let not the schism with me cause you to sin so that there befall you that which befell the folk of Noah and the folk of Hud, and the folk of Salih; and the folk of Lot are not far off from you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O my people, do not let your defiance toward me lead you to be visited by the like of what was visited on the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih, and the people of Lot are not distant from you.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And my nation, do not let your breaking with me bring upon you something similar to that which struck the nation of Noah, Hood, and Salih and it is not long since the people of Lot (were punished).

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And O my people, let not [your] dissension from me cause you to be struck by that similar to what struck the people of Noah or the people of Hud or the people of Salih. And the people of Lot are not from you far away.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My people, do not let your opposition to me lead you to commit sins or make you suffer what the people of Noah, Hud, and Salih suffered. Remember that the people of Lot were destroyed not very long ago.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And, O my people! let not opposition to me make you guilty so that there may befall you the like of what befell the people of Nuh, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih, nor are the people of Lut far off from you;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: O my people, do not let your opposition to me bring upon you a fate similar to the peoples of Noah or Hud or Salih; nor is it long since the people of Lot were punished!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And O my people! let not my dissent (from you) cause you to sin, lest ye suffer a fate similar to that of the people of Noah or of Hud or of Salih, nor are the people of Lut far off from you!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waya qawmi la yajrimannakum shiqaqee an yuseebakum mithlu ma asaba qawma noohin aw qawma hoodin aw qawma salihin wama qawmu lootin minkum bibaAAeedin

90.arabic: وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى رَحِيمٌ وَدُودٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Beg your Lord to forgive you, and turn to Him. Indeed my Lord is compassionate and loving."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Ask forgiveness from your Lord and then incline towards Him in repentance; indeed my Lord is Most Merciful, Most Affectionate.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And ask forgiveness of your Lord, then repent to Him; surely my Lord is All-compassionate, All-loving.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And ask forgiveness of your Lord, and then repent unto him; verily my Lord is Merciful, Loving.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And ask forgiveness of your Lord and turn unto Him in repentance. Verily, my Lord is Most Merciful, Most Loving."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “And ask your Lord for forgiveness, and repent to Him. My Lord is Merciful and Loving.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Seek the forgiveness of your Lord and turn to Him in repentance. Surely my Lord is Ever Merciful, Most Loving.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And ask forgiveness of your Lord and turn unto Him in repentance. Verily, my Lord is Most Merciful, Most Loving."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Ask pardon of your Lord and then turn unto Him (repentant). Lo! my Lord is Merciful, Loving.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Plead with your Lord for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. My Lord is indeed all-merciful, all-affectionate.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Ask the forgiveness of your Lord and turn to Him in repentance. Indeed, my Lord is the Most Merciful, Loving'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Seek forgiveness from your Lord and turn to Him in repentance. My Lord is certainly All-merciful and Loving."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And ask forgiveness of your Lord, then turn to Him; surely my Lord is Merciful, Loving-kind.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Seek forgiveness of your Lord and turn to Him in repentance. For my Lord is indeed merciful and loving."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But ask forgiveness of your Lord, and turn unto Him (in repentance): For my Lord is indeed full of mercy and loving-kindness."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waistaghfiroo rabbakum thumma tooboo ilayhi inna rabbee raheemun wadoodun

91.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ مَا نَفْقَهُ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِينَا ضَعِيفًا ۖ وَلَوْلَا رَهْطُكَ لَرَجَمْنَـٰكَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْنَا بِعَزِيزٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Shu'aib, much of what you say is meaningless to us, and then (for sure) you are powerless among us. But for your clan we would have stoned you to death. You have no power over us.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “O Shuaib! We do not understand most of what you say, and indeed we perceive you weak among us; were it not for your relatives, we would have stoned you; and in our sight, you have no respect at all.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Shuaib, we do not understand much of what thou sayest. Truly we see thee weak among us; but for thy tribe we would have stoned thee; for thou art not strong against us.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: O Shu'aib! we understanand not much of that which thou sayest, and verily we see thee weak among us, and were it not for thy company we had surely stoned thee, and thou art not among us mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "O Shu'aib! We do not understand much of what you say, and we see you a weak (man, it is said that he was a blind man) among us. Were it not for your family, we should certainly have stoned you and you are not powerful against us."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “O Shuaib, we do not understand much of what you say, and we see that you are weak among us. Were it not for your tribe, we would have stoned you. You are of no value to us.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They said: 'O Shu'ayb! We do not understand much of what you say. Indeed we see you weak in our midst. Were it not for your kinsmen, we would surely have stoned you for you have no strength to overpower us.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "O Shu`ayb! We do not understand much of what you say, and we see you weak among us. Were it not for your family, you would have been stoned, and you are not powerful against us."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: O Shu'eyb! We understand not much of that thou tellest, and lo! we do behold thee weak among us. But for thy family, we should have stoned thee, for thou art not strong against us.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘O Shu‘ayb, we do not understand much of what you say. Indeed we see that you are weak amongst us, and were it not for your tribe, we would have stoned you, and you are not a formidable [challenge] for us.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They said 'Shu'aib, we do not understand much of what you say to us. Indeed, we see you weak among us. If it was not for your tribe we would have stoned you because you are not strong against us'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "O Shu'ayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and indeed, we consider you among us as weak. And if not for your family, we would have stoned you [to death]; and you are not to us one respected."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "Shu'ayb, we do not understand much of what you say, but we know that you are weak among us. Had it not been for our respect of your tribe, we would have stoned you to death; you are not very dear to us".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: O Shu'aib! we do not understand much of what you say and most surely we see you to be weak among us, and were it not for your family we would surely stone you, and you are not mighty against us.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They replied, "Shu'ayb, we do not understand much of what you say. In fact, we see that you are powerless among us.Were it not for your clan, we would have stoned you, for you are not strong against us."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "O Shu'aib! much of what thou sayest we do not understand! In fact among us we see that thou hast no strength! Were it not for thy family, we should certainly have stoned thee! for thou hast among us no great position!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ya shuAAaybu ma nafqahu katheeran mimma taqoolu wainna lanaraka feena daAAeefan walawla rahtuka larajamnaka wama anta AAalayna biAAazeezin

92.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَهْطِىٓ أَعَزُّ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱتَّخَذْتُمُوهُ وَرَآءَكُمْ ظِهْرِيًّا ۖ إِنَّ رَبِّى بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "My clan seems mightier to you than God whom you neglect and push behind your backs. Surely what you do is within the power of my Lord.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O my people! Is the pressure upon you from my relatives, worth more than Allah? And you put Him* behind your backs; indeed whatever you do is all within my Lords’ control.” (* His command / my preaching.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'O my people, is my tribe stronger against you than God? And Him -- have you taken Him as something to be thrust behind you? My Lord encompasses the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: O my people! is my company mightier with you than Allah? Him ye have cast behind your backs neglected; verily my Lord is of that which ye work Encompasser.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Is then my family of more weight with you than Allah? And you have cast Him away behind your backs. Verily, my Lord is surrounding all that you do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O my people, is my tribe more important to you than God? And you have turned your backs on Him? My Lord comprehends everything you do.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Shu'ayb said: 'My people! Are my kinsmen mightier with you than Allah that you (hold the kinsmen in awe while) you cast Allah behind your back? Surely my Lord encompasses all what you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "O my people! Is then my family of more weight with you than Allah And you have cast Him away behind your backs. Verily, my Lord is surrounding all that you do."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O my people! Is my family more to be honoured by you than Allah? and ye put Him behind you, neglected! Lo! my Lord surroundeth what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O my people! Is my tribe more formidable in your sight than Allah, to whom you pay no regard? Indeed my Lord comprehends whatever you do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He said: 'My nation, is my tribe stronger against you than Allah? You have taken Him to be thrown behind you, my Lord encompasses all that you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "O my people, is my family more respected for power by you than Allah? But you put Him behind your backs [in neglect]. Indeed, my Lord is encompassing of what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He asked them, "My people, is my tribe more respectable to you than God whom you have completely ignored? My Lord certainly has full control over your deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O my people! is my family more esteemed by you than Allah? And you neglect Him as a thing cast behind your back; surely my Lord encompasses what you do:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "My people, is my tribe mightier to you than God? You put Him behind you, turning your backs on Him! Surely, my Lord encompasses all that you do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people! is then my family of more consideration with you than Allah? For ye cast Him away behind your backs (with contempt). But verily my Lord encompasseth on all sides all that ye do!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya qawmi arahtee aAAazzu AAalaykum mina Allahi waittakhathtumoohu waraakum thihriyyan inna rabbee bima taAAmaloona muheetun

93.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّى عَـٰمِلٌ ۖ سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَمَنْ هُوَ كَـٰذِبٌ ۖ وَٱرْتَقِبُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُمْ رَقِيبٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do on your part what you can, O people, I will do what I will. You will come to know who suffers the punishment that would put him to shame, and who is the liar. So watch; I am watching with you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And O my people! Keep on with your works in your positions, I am doing mine; very soon you will come to know – upon whom comes the punishment that will disgrace him, and who is the liar; and wait – I too am waiting with you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O my people, act according to your station; I am acting; and certainly you will know to whom will come the chastisement degrading him, and who is a liar. And be upon the watch; I shall be with you, watching.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And, O my people! work according to your condition, verily I am going to work in my way; presently ye shall know on whom cometh a torment humiliating him and who is a liar. And Watch, verily I also am with you a watcher.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And O my people! Act according to your ability and way, and I am acting (on my way). You will come to know who it is on whom descends the torment that will cover him with ignominy, and who is a liar! And watch you! Verily, I too am watching with you."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “O my people, do as you may, and so will I. You will know to whom will come a punishment that will shame him, and who is a liar. So look out; I am on the lookout with you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My people! Go on working according to your way and I will keep working (according to mine). Soon you will come to know who will be afflicted by a humiliating chastisement, and who is proved a liar. And watch, I shall also watch with you.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And O my people! Act according to your ability, and I am acting. You will come to know who it is on whom descends the torment that will cover him with ignominy, and who is a liar! And watch you! Verily, I too am watching with you."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And, O my people! Act according to your power, lo! I (too) am acting. Ye will soon know on whom there cometh a doom that will abase him, and who it is that lieth. And watch! Lo! I am a watcher with you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O my people! Act according to your ability; I too am acting. Soon you will know who will be overtaken by a punishment that will disgrace him, and who is a liar. So be on the watch; I too will be watching along with you.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: My nation, do according to your position; as I am doing; and certainly you will know to whom the degrading punishment will come and who is a liar. Be watchful; I shall be watching with you'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And O my people, work according to your position; indeed, I am working. You are going to know to whom will come a punishment that will disgrace him and who is a liar. So watch; indeed, I am with you a watcher, [awaiting the outcome]."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: My people, do as you wish and I will do (as I believe). You will soon know who will suffer a humiliating torment and who was the one telling lies. Wait and I too will be waiting with you."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And, O my people! act according to your ability, I too am acting; you will come to know soon who it is on whom will light the punishment that will disgrace him and who it is that is a liar, and watch, surely I too am watching with you.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: My people, do what you will and so shall I. You will certainly come to know who will receive a punishment to disgrace him, and who is a liar. Wait on; I shall wait on with you."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And O my people! Do whatever ye can: I will do (my part): Soon will ye know who it is on whom descends the penalty of ignominy; and who is a liar! and watch ye! for I too am watching with you!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waya qawmi iAAmaloo AAala makanatikum innee AAamilun sawfa taAAlamoona man yateehi AAathabun yukhzeehi waman huwa kathibun wairtaqiboo inne maAAakum raqeebun

94.arabic: وَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا شُعَيْبًا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَأَخَذَتِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَـٰرِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And when Our word came to pass, We rescued Shu'aib and those who believed, with him, by Our grace, but those who were wicked were seized by a punishment from heaven, and lay overturned in their homes in the morning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Our command came, We rescued Shuaib and the Muslims who were with him by Our mercy; and the terrible scream seized the unjust – so at morning they remained lying flattened in their homes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Our command came, We delivered Shuaib and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us, and the evildoers were seized by the Cry, and morning found them in their habitations fallen prostrate

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when our decree came to pass, We delivered Shu'aib and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us, and the shout overtook those who did wrong, so they lay in their dwellings crouching.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when Our Commandment came, We saved Shu'aib and those who believed with him by a Mercy from Us. And As-Saihah (torment - awful cry, etc.) seized the wrong-doers, and they lay (dead) prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when Our command came, We saved Shuaib and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and the Blast struck the wrongdoers, and they became motionless bodies in their homes.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And when Our command came to pass, We delivered Shu'ayb and those who shared his faith, through Our mercy, and the Blast seized those who were engaged in wrong-doing, so they lay lifeless in their homes

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when Our commandment came, We saved Shu`ayb and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us. And As-Sayhah (awful cry) seized the wrongdoers, and they lay (Jathimin) in their homes.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Our commandment came to pass We saved Shu'eyb and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us; and the (Awful) Cry seized those who did injustice, and morning found them prostrate in their dwellings,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Our edict came, We delivered Shu‘ayb and the faithful who were with him by a mercy from Us. And the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers, whereat they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Our command came We saved Shu'aib through Our Mercy, together with those who believed. (Then) the evildoers were seized by a Shout, and when morning came they were crouched in their dwellings, dead,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when Our command came, We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us. And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Our decree came to pass, We mercifully saved Shu'ayb and his faithful followers. A blast struck the unjust and left them in their homes, lying motionless on their faces,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Our decree came to pass We delivered Shu'aib, and those who believed with him by mercy from Us, and the rumbling overtook those who were unjust so they became motionless bodies in their abodes,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Our command came, We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him as a mercy from Ourself, while the blast overtook the wrongdoers, so that they lay dead in their homes,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When Our decree issued, We saved Shu'aib and those who believed with him, by (special) mercy from Ourselves: But the (mighty) blast did seize the wrong-doers, and they lay prostrate in their homes by the morning,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walamma jaa amruna najjayna shuAAayban waallatheena amanoo maAAahu birahmatin minna waakhathati allatheena thalamoo alssayhatu faasbahoo fee diyarihim jathimeena

95.arabic: كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّمَدْيَنَ كَمَا بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As though they had not dwelt there at all. Beware! Condemmed were the people of Midian as those of Thamud had been before them!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: As if they had never lived there; away with the Madyan, just as the Thamud were removed afar!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: as if they had never dwelt there: 'So away with Midian, even as Thamood was done away!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: As though they had never lived at ease therein. Lo! a far removal for Madyan, even as Thamud were removed afar!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As if they had never lived there! So away with Madyan (Midian)! As away with Thamud! (All these nations were destroyed).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As though they never flourished therein. Away with Median, as was done away with Thamood.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: as though they had never dwelt in them before. Lo! Away with (the people of) Midian, even as the Thamud were done away with!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As if they had never lived there! So away with Madyan just as Thamud went away!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As though they had not dwelt there. A far removal for Midian, even as Thamud had been removed afar!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: as if they had never lived there. Behold, away with Midian!—just as Thamud was done away with!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: as if they had never have lived there. Begone, the nation of Midian, just as Thamood are gone.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As if they had never prospered therein. Then, away with Madyan as Thamud was taken away.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: as though they had never existed. How far from (the mercy of God) had the people of Midian gone, just as those of Thamud?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: As though they had never dwelt in them; now surely perdition overtook Madyan as had perished Samood.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: as though they had never dwelt therein. Oh, away with the people of Midian, just as happened with the Thamud!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: As if they had never dwelt and flourished there! Ah! Behold! How the Madyan were removed (from sight) as were removed the Thamud!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kaan lam yaghnaw feeha ala buAAdan limadyana kama baAAidat thamoodu

96.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We sent Moses with Our signs and full authority

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed We sent Moosa with Our revelations and a clear domination.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We sent Moses with Our signs, and a manifest authority,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly We sent Musa with Our signs and a manifest warranty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed We sent Musa (Moses) with Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) and a manifest authority;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We sent Moses with Our signs and a clear mandate.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And indeed We sent Moses with Our signs and with a clear authority

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed We sent Musa with Our Ayat and a manifest authority.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And verily We sent Moses with Our revelations and a clear warrant

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and a manifest authority

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We sent Moses with Our signs and with clear authority

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We did certainly send Moses with Our signs and a clear authority

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent Moses to the Pharaoh and his nobles with Our miracles and a clear authority.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly We sent Musa with Our communications and a clear authority,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We sent forth Moses with Our signs and with manifest authority,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And we sent Moses, with Our Clear (Signs) and an authority manifest,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad arsalna moosa biayatina wasultanin mubeenin

97.arabic: إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ فَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۖ وَمَآ أَمْرُ فِرْعَوْنَ بِرَشِيدٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: To the Pharaoh and his nobles, but they followed the bidding of Pharaoh, though the bidding of Pharaoh was unrightful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Towards Firaun and his court members, thereupon they followed the commands of Firaun; and the work of Firaun was not proper.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: to Pharaoh and his Council; but they followed Pharaoh's command, and Pharaoh's command was not right-minded.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Unto Firawn and his chiefs. But they followed the commandment of Fir'awn, and the commandment of Fir'awn was not right- minded.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: To Fir'aun (Pharaoh) and his chiefs, but they followed the command of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and the command of Fir'aun (Pharaoh) was no right guide.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To Pharaoh and his nobles, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: to Pharaoh and his nobles. But they obeyed the command of Pharaoh even though Pharaoh's command was not rightly-directed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: To Fir`awn and his chiefs, but they followed the command of Fir`awn, and the command of Fir`awn was no right guide.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Unto Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they did follow the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was no right guide.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: to Pharaoh and his elite, but they followed Pharaoh’s dictates, and Pharaoh’s dictates were not right-minded.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: to Pharaoh and his Council. But they followed the command of Pharaoh, and Pharaoh's command was not rightminded.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: To Pharaoh and his establishment, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not [at all] discerning.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They followed the order of the Pharaoh but Pharaoh's orders were evil.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: To Firon and his chiefs, but they followed the bidding of Firon, and Firon's bidding was not right-directing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: to Pharaoh and his nobles. But they followed the command of Pharaoh and the command of Pharaoh was not rightly directed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Unto Pharaoh and his chiefs: but they followed the command of Pharaoh and the command of Pharaoh was no right (guide).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ila firAAawna wamalaihi faittabaAAoo amra firAAawna wama amru firAAawna birasheedin

98.arabic: يَقْدُمُ قَوْمَهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فَأَوْرَدَهُمُ ٱلنَّارَ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْوِرْدُ ٱلْمَوْرُودُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He shall be at the head of his people on the Day of Resurrection, and drive them into Hell like cattle driven to water -- what an evil watering-place to reach!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He will lead his people on the Day of Resurrection, therefore landing them into hell; and what a wretched place to land into!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He shall go before his people on the Day of Resurrection, and will have led them down to the Fire-evil the watering-place to be led down to!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He shall head his people on the Day of Resurrection and cause them to descend into the Fire, ill is the descent, descended!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He will go ahead of his people on the Day of Resurrection, and will lead them into the Fire, and evil indeed is the place to which they are led.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection, and will lead them into the Fire. Miserable is the place he placed them in.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He shall stand at the head of his people on the Day of Resurrection, and will bring them down to the Fire. What a wretched destination to be led to!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He will go ahead of his people on the Day of Resurrection, and will lead them into the Fire, and evil indeed is the place to which they are led.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He will go before his people on the Day of Resurrection and will lead them to the Fire for watering-place. Ah, hapless is the watering-place (whither they are) led.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: On the Day of Resurrection he will lead his people and conduct them into the Fire: an evil goal for the incoming!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He shall go before his nation on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire. Evil is the wateringplace to be led down to!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire; and wretched is the place to which they are led.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On the Day of Judgment he will lead his people down into the hell fire. His leadership is evil and terrible is the place to which he leads!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He shall lead his people on the resurrection day, and bring them down to the fire; and evil the place to which they are brought.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He shall stand at the head of his people on the Day of Resurrection, and shall lead them into the Fire. Evil is the place to which they shall be led.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He will go before his people on the Day of Judgment, and lead them into the Fire (as cattle are led to water): But woeful indeed will be the place to which they are led!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yaqdumu qawmahu yawma alqiyamati faawradahumu alnnara wabisa alwirdu almawroodu

99.arabic: وَأُتْبِعُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِۦ لَعْنَةً وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ بِئْسَ ٱلرِّفْدُ ٱلْمَرْفُودُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Damned will they be in this world, and on the Day of Doom how evil the gift that they will receive!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And a curse followed them in the world, and on the Day of Resurrection; what a wretched gift is what they received.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And there was sent following after them in this world a curse, and upon the Day of Resurrection -- evil the offering to be offered!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they were followed in this world by a curse and so they will be on the Day of Resurrection, ill is the present presented!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They were pursued by a curse in this (deceiving life of this world) and (so they will be pursued by a curse) on the Day of Resurrection. How bad is the curse (in this world) pursued by another curse (in the Hereafter).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They were followed by a curse in this, and on the Day of Resurrection. Miserable is the path they followed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They were pursued by a curse in this world and so will they be on the Day of Resurrection. What an evil reward will they receive!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They were pursued by a curse in this (deceiving life of this world) and (so they will be pursued by a curse) on the Day of Resurrection, evil indeed is the gift granted.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A curse is made to follow them in the world and on the Day of Resurrection. Hapless is the gift (that will be) given (them).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They are pursued by a curse in this [world], as well as on the Day of Resurrection; evil is the award conferred [upon them]!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: A curse was sent to follow them in this world and then upon them on the Day of Resurrection. Evil is the offering to be offered.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they were followed in this [world] with a curse and on the Day of Resurrection. And wretched is the gift which is given.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They are condemned in this world and in the life to come. Evil is the gift and the recipient!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they are overtaken by curse in this (world), and on the resurrection day, evil the gift which shall be given.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: A curse followed them in this world, and shall follow them on the Day of Resurrection. What a foul gift to be given!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And they are followed by a curse in this (life) and on the Day of Judgment: and woeful is the gift which shall be given (unto them)!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaotbiAAoo fee hathihi laAAnatan wayawma alqiyamati bisa alrrifdu almarfoodu

100.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّهُۥ عَلَيْكَ ۖ مِنْهَا قَآئِمٌ وَحَصِيدٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: These are a few accounts of settlements that We narrate to you. Some still survive, and some have been mowed down.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: These are the tidings of the townships, which We relate to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) – some of them still stand and some are cut off.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That is of the tidings of the cities We relate to thee; some of them are standing and some stubble.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That is from the stories of the Cities which We recount unto thee: of them some are standing and some mown down.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: These are some of the news of the (population of) towns which We relate unto you (O Muhammad SAW); of them, some are standing, and some have been (already) reaped.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: These are of the reports of the towns—We relate them to you. Some are still standing, and some have withered away.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: That is an account of some towns which We recount to you. Of them some are still standing and some have been mown down.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That is some of the news of the (population of) towns which We relate unto you; of them, some are (still) standing, and some have been (already) reaped.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That is (something) of the tidings of the townships (which were destroyed of old). We relate it unto thee (Muhammad). Some of them are standing and some (already) reaped.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: These are from the accounts of the townships, which We recount to you. Of them, there are some that still stand, and some that have been mown down.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: That, which We have related to you, is the news of the villages; some of them still stand, whereas others are stubble.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That is from the news of the cities, which We relate to you; of them, some are [still] standing and some are [as] a harvest [mowed down].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Such were the stories of the nations of the past which We tell to you, (Muhammad). Some of them were destroyed and some of them have survived.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This is an account of (the fate of) the towns which We relate to you; of them are some that stand and (others) mown down.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We relate to you such accounts of earlier towns: some of them are still standing; while others have ceased to exist;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: These are some of the stories of communities which We relate unto thee: of them some are standing, and some have been mown down (by the sickle of time).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika min anbai alqura naqussuhu AAalayka minha qaimun wahaseedun

101.arabic: وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ فَمَآ أَغْنَتْ عَنْهُمْ ءَالِهَتُهُمُ ٱلَّتِى يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ لَّمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا زَادُوهُمْ غَيْرَ تَتْبِيبٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We did not wrong them; they wronged themselves. When your Lord's chastisement descended upon them, their gods, on whom they called apart from God, were not of the least avail, and all they did was only to add to their destruction.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We did not oppress them at all, but it is they who wronged themselves – therefore their deities, whom they worshipped other than Allah, did not in the least benefit them when your Lord’s command came; and due to them, they increased nothing but ruin.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We wronged them not, but they wronged themselves; their gods availed them not that they called upon, apart from God, anything, when the command of thy Lord came; and they increased them not, save in destruction.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We wronged them not but they wronged themselves. So their gods, whom they called upon beside Allah, availed them not in aught, when there came the decree of thy Lord, and they added unto them naught but perdition.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: We wronged them not, but they wronged themselves. So their aliha (gods), other than Allah, whom they invoked, profited them naught when there came the Command of your Lord, nor did they add aught (to their lot) but destruction.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. Their gods, whom they invoked besides God, availed them nothing when the command of your Lord arrived. In fact, they added only to their ruin.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We did not wrong them; it is rather they who wronged themselves. And when the command of your Lord came to pass, the gods besides Allah whom they had called upon, did not avail them in the least. They added nothing to them except ruin.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: We wronged them not, but they wronged themselves. So their gods whom they call upon besides Allah, profited them naught when there came the command of your Lord, nor did they add aught to them but destruction.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We wronged them not, but they did wrong themselves; and their gods on whom they call beside Allah availed them naught when came thy Lord's command; they added to them naught save ruin.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. When your Lord’s edict came, their gods whom they would invoke besides Allah were of no avail to them in any wise, and they did not increase them in anything but ruin.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We did not wrong them, rather, they wronged themselves. Their gods that they called upon, other than Allah, did not help them when your Lord's command came they did not increased them except in their destruction.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. And they were not availed at all by their gods which they invoked other than Allah when there came the command of your Lord. And they did not increase them in other than ruin.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We were not unjust to them but they were unjust to themselves. The idols which they worshipped instead of God were of no help to them when Our decree came to pass. The idols only brought about their destruction.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We did not do them injustice, but they were unjust to themselves, so their gods whom they called upon besides Allah did not avail them aught when the decree of your Lord came to pass; and they added but to their ruin.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We did not wrong them; they wronged themselves; the deities they called on besides God availed them nothing: when God's command came upon them, they only added to their ruin.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It was not We that wronged them: They wronged their own souls: the deities, other than Allah, whom they invoked, profited them no whit when there issued the decree of thy Lord: Nor did they add aught (to their lot) but perdition!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama thalamnahum walakin thalamoo anfusahum fama aghnat AAanhum alihatuhumu allatee yadAAoona min dooni Allahi min shayin lamma jaa amru rabbika wama zadoohum ghayra tatbeebin

102.arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَخْذُ رَبِّكَ إِذَآ أَخَذَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَهِىَ ظَـٰلِمَةٌ ۚ إِنَّ أَخْذَهُۥٓ أَلِيمٌ شَدِيدٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Such is the punishment of your Lord when He seizes human settlements in the acts of wickedness. Surely His hold is grievous and terrible.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And similar is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the townships upon their injustice; indeed His seizure is painful, severe.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Such is the seizing of thy Lord, when He seizes the cities that are evildoing; surely His seizing is painful, terrible.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And even such is the overtaking of thy Lord when He overtaketh the cities while they are wrong-doers; verily His overtaking is afflictive, severe.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Such is the Seizure of your Lord when He seizes the (population of) towns while they are doing wrong. Verily, His Seizure is painful, and severe.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Such is the grip of your Lord when He seizes the towns in the midst of their sins. His grip is most painful, most severe.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Such is the seizing of your Lord that when He does seize the towns immersed in wrong-doing, His seizing is painful, terrible.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Such is the punishment of your Lord when He seizes the (population of) towns while they are doing wrong. Verily, His punishment is painful (and) severe.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Even thus is the grasp of thy Lord when He graspeth the townships while they are doing wrong. Lo! His grasp is painful, very strong.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Such is the seizing of your Lord when He seizes the townships that are wrongdoing. Indeed His seizing is painful and severe.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Such is the seizing of your Lord, when He seizes the evildoing villages. His seizing is painful, stern.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And thus is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the cities while they are committing wrong. Indeed, His seizure is painful and severe.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Thus was the punishment of your Lord when He punished the unjust people of the towns. The punishment of your Lord is certainly severe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And such is the punishment of your Lord when He punishes the towns while they are unjust; surely His punishment is painful, severe.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Such is the punishment of your Lord when He seizes the towns in the midst of their sins: His punishing grip is terrible and severe.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
