translations.text: Such is the chastisement of thy Lord when He chastises communities in the midst of their wrong: grievous, indeed, and severe is His chastisement.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wakathalika akhthu rabbika itha akhatha alqura wahiya thalimatun inna akhthahu aleemun shadeedun

103.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّمَنْ خَافَ عَذَابَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَّجْمُوعٌ لَّهُ ٱلنَّاسُ وَذَٰلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَّشْهُودٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: In this surely is a sign for him who fears the torment of the Hereafter, the day when mankind will be assembled together, which will be a day when all things would become evident.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed in this is a sign for one who fears the punishment of the Hereafter; this is the day on which everyone will be gathered, and this is the Day of Attendance.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely in that is a sign for him who fears the chastisement in the world to come; that is a day mankind are to be gathered to, a day to witness,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily herein is a sign unto him who feareth the torment of the Hereafter. That is a Day whereon mankind shall be gathered together, and that is a Day to be witnessed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Indeed in that (there) is a sure lesson for those who fear the torment of the Hereafter. That is a Day whereon mankind will be gathered together, and that is a Day when all (the dwellers of the heavens and the earth) will be present.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In that is a sign for whoever fears the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which humanity will be gathered together—that is a Day to be witnessed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely in that is a sign for him who fears the chastisement of the Hereafter. That will be a Day when all men shall be mustered together; that will be a Day when whatever happens shall be witnessed by all.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Indeed in that (there) is a sure lesson for those who fear the torment of the Hereafter. That is a Day whereon mankind will be gathered together, and that is a Day when all will be present.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! herein verily there is a portent for those who fear the doom of the Hereafter. That is a day unto which mankind will be gathered, and that is a day that will be witnessed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: There is indeed a sign in that for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a day on which all mankind will be gathered, and it is a day witnessed [by all creatures].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, for he who fears the punishment of the Everlasting Life that is a sign. That is a Day on which everyone shall be assembled. That shall be a witnessed Day.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed in that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which the people will be collected, and that is a Day [which will be] witnessed.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: In this there is, certainly, a lesson for those who fear the torment of the next life in which all people will be gathered together

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely there is a sign in this for him who fears the chastisement of the hereafter; this is a day on which the people shall be gathered together and this is a day that shall be witnessed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: In that is a sign for him who fears the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which mankind shall be gathered together, a Day when all will be present.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: In that is a Sign for those who fear the penalty of the Hereafter: that is a Day for which mankind will be gathered together: that will be a Day of Testimony.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan liman khafa AAathaba alakhirati thalika yawmun majmooAAun lahu alnnasu wathalika yawmun mashhoodun

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We are deferring it only for a time ordained.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We do not postpone it except for a fixed term.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and We shall not postpone it, save to a term reckoned;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We defer it not but to a term determined.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We delay it only for a term (already) fixed.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We only postpone it until a predetermined time.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Nor shall We withhold it except till an appointed term.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We delay it only for a term (already) fixed.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We defer it only to a term already reckoned.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And We do not defer it but for a determinate term.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We shall not postpone it except until a counted term.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We do not delay it except for a limited term.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: during a single day. We have deferred this day for an appointed time.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We do not delay it but to an appointed term.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We will only postpone it until a predetermined time,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor shall We delay it but for a term appointed.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama nuakhkhiruhu illa liajalin maAAdoodin

105.arabic: يَوْمَ يَأْتِ لَا تَكَلَّمُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ فَمِنْهُمْ شَقِىٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The day it comes no soul will dare say a word but by His leave; and some will be wretched, some will be blessed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When that day comes, no one will speak without His permission; so among them are the ill-fated and the fortunate.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: the day it comes, no soul shall speak save by His leave; some of them shall be wretched and some happy.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The day it cometh no soul shall speak save by His leave: then of them some shall be wretched and some blessed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: On the Day when it comes, no person shall speak except by His (Allah's) Leave. Some among them will be wretched and (others) blessed.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: On the Day when it arrives, no soul will speak without His permission. Some will be miserable, and some will be happy.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And when the appointed Day comes, no one shall even dare to speak except by the leave of Allah. Then some will be declared wretched, others blessed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: On the Day when it comes, no person shall speak except by His (Allah's) leave. Some among them will be wretched and (others) blessed.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: On the day when it cometh no soul will speak except by His permission; some among them will be wretched, (others) glad.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The day it comes, no one shall speak except by His leave. [On that day,] some of them will be wretched and [some] felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When that Day arrives no soul shall speak except by His permission. Some shall be wretched, and others happy.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The Day it comes no soul will speak except by His permission. And among them will be the wretched and the prosperous.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On the Day of Judgment no one will speak without the permission of God. Some will be condemned and others blessed.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: On the day when it shall come, no soul shall speak except with His permission, then (some) of them shall be unhappy and (others) happy.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and when that Day arrives, no soul shall speak but by His leave. Among those some shall be damned, and others shall be blessed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The day it arrives, no soul shall speak except by His leave: of those (gathered) some will be wretched and some will be blessed.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yawma yati la takallamu nafsun illa biithnihi faminhum shaqiyyun wasaAAeedin

106.arabic: فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ شَقُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلنَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌ وَشَهِيقٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And those who are doomed, will be in Hell: For them will be sighing and sobbing,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So those who are ill-fated, are in the fire – they will bray like donkeys in it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: As for the wretched, they shall be in the Fire, wherein there shall be for them moaning and sighing, therein dwelling forever,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: As for those who shall be wretched. they shall be in the Fire, wherein for them shall be panting and roaring.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As for those who are wretched, they will be in the Fire, sighing in a high and low tone.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who are miserable, they will be in the Fire. They will have therein sighing and wailing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for the wretched, they shall be in the Fire, and in it they shall sigh and groan.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As for those who are wretched, they will be in the Fire, in it they will experience Zafir and Shahiq.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As for those who will be wretched (on that day) they will be in the Fire; sighing and wailing will be their portion therein,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for the wretched, they shall be in the Fire: their lot therein will be groaning and wailing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The wretched shall be (cast) in the Fire where they will moan and sigh,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As for those who were [destined to be] wretched, they will be in the Fire. For them therein is [violent] exhaling and inhaling.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The condemned ones will live in hell fire, sighing and groaning

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So as to those who are unhappy, they shall be in the fire; for them shall be sighing and groaning in it:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The wretched ones will be in the Fire sighing and groaning,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who are wretched shall be in the Fire: There will be for them therein (nothing but) the heaving of sighs and sobs:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faamma allatheena shaqoo fafee alnnari lahum feeha zafeerun washaheequn

107.arabic: خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ فَعَّالٌ لِّمَا يُرِيدُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Where they will dwell so long as heaven and earth endure, unless your Lord will otherwise. Verily your Lord does as He wills.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Remaining in it as long as the heavens and the earth remain, except as much as your Lord willed*; indeed your Lord may do whatever, whenever, He wills. (* Remaining forever even after the heavens and the earth are destroyed.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: so long as the heavens and earth abide, save as thy Lord will; surely thy Lord accomplishes what He desires.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Abiders they shall be therein, so long as the heavens and the earth remain, save as thy Lord may will. Verily thy Lord is the Doer of whatsoever He intendeth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will dwell therein for all the time that the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills. Verily, your Lord is the doer of what He wills.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Remaining therein for as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills. Your Lord is Doer of whatever He wills.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They shall abide in it as long as the heavens and the earth endure, unless your Lord may will otherwise. Surely your Lord does whatsoever He wills.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will dwell therein for all the time that the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills. Verily, your Lord is the doer of whatsoever He intends (or wills).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Abiding there so long as the heavens and the earth endure save for that which thy Lord willeth. Lo! thy Lord is Doer of what He will.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They shall remain in it for as long as the heavens and the earth endure—except what your Lord may wish; indeed your Lord does whatever He desires.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and there they shall live for ever, as long as the heavens and the earth endure, and as your Lord wills. Indeed, your Lord accomplishes whatsoever He will.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [They will be] abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will. Indeed, your Lord is an effecter of what He intends.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: for as long as the heavens and the earth exist, unless your Lord decides otherwise. Your Lord will certainly accomplish whatever He wants.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Abiding therein so long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord please; surely your Lord is the mighty doer of what He intends.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: remaining in it timelessly, for ever, as long as the heavens and earth endure, except as your Lord wills. Your Lord carries out whatever He wills.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will dwell therein for all the time that the heavens and the earth endure, except as thy Lord willeth: for thy Lord is the (sure) accomplisher of what He planneth.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Khalideena feeha ma damati alssamawatu waalardu illa ma shaa rabbuka inna rabbaka faAAAAalun lima yureedu

108.arabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۖ عَطَآءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who are blessed will be in Paradise, where they will dwell so long as heaven and earth survive, unless your Lord wills otherwise: This will be a gift uninterrupted.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So those who are fortunate are in Paradise – abiding in it as long as the heavens and the earth remain, except as much as your Lord willed; this is the everlasting favour.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And as for the happy, they shall be in Paradise, therein dwelling forever, so long as the heavens and earth abide, save as thy Lord will -- for a gift unbroken.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And as for those who shall be blest, they shall be in the Garden, as abiders therein so long as the heavens and the earth remain, save as thy Lord may will. a gift unending.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those who are blessed, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein for all the time that the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord will, a gift without an end.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And as for those who are happy, they will be in Paradise, remaining therein for as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills—a reward without end.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And as for those who are blessed, they shall abide in the Garden as long as the heavens and the earth endure, unless your Lord may will otherwise. They shall enjoy an unceasing gift.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those who are blessed, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein for all the time that the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills: a gift without an end.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And as for those who will be glad (that day) they will be in the Garden, abiding there so long as the heavens and the earth endure save for that which thy Lord willeth: a gift unfailing.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for the felicitous, they will be in paradise. They will remain in it for as long as the heavens and the earth endure—except what your Lord may wish—an endless bounty.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As for the happy, they shall live in Paradise for ever, so as long as the heavens and the earth endure, and as your Lord wills an unbroken gift.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And as for those who were [destined to be] prosperous, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will - a bestowal uninterrupted.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The blessed ones will live in Paradise as long as the heavens and the earth exist, unless your Lord decides to grant endless rewards to whomever He wants.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And as to those who are made happy, they shall be in the garden, abiding in it as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord please; a gift which shall never be cut off.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who are blessed shall abide in the Garden; they will dwell therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills. Such bounty shall be unending.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And those who are blessed shall be in the Garden: They will dwell therein for all the time that the heavens and the earth endure, except as thy Lord willeth: a gift without break.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waamma allatheena suAAidoo fafee aljannati khalideena feeha ma damati alssamawatu waalardu illa ma shaa rabbuka AAataan ghayra majthoothin

109.arabic: فَلَا تَكُ فِى مِرْيَةٍ مِّمَّا يَعْبُدُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ۚ مَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُهُم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ وَإِنَّا لَمُوَفُّوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ غَيْرَ مَنقُوصٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, you should not entertain any doubt about those whom they worship: They only worship what their fathers had worshipped before them. We shall verily give them their meed without diminution.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So O listener (followers of this Prophet), do not fall into doubt by what these disbelievers worship; they only worship just as their forefathers worshipped before; and indeed we shall pay them their due in full, undiminished.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So be thou not in doubt concerning what these men serve; they serve only as their fathers served before; and We shall surely pay them in full their portion undiminished.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So be not thou in dubitation concerning that which these people worship. They worship not save as their fathers worshipped afore; and verily We will repay unto them in full their portion, undiminished.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So be not in doubt (O Muhammad SAW) as to what these (pagans and polytheists) men worship. They worship nothing but what their fathers worshipped before (them). And verily, We shall repay them in full their portion without diminution.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So be not in doubt regarding what these people worship. They worship only as their ancestors worshiped before. We will pay them their due in full, without any reduction.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: [O Prophet!] Have no doubt about what they worship. For they worship what their fathers worshipped before. And (yet) We shall grant them their due portion in full, diminishing of it nothing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So be not in doubt as to what these people (pagans and polytheists) worship. They worship nothing but what their fathers worshipped before (them). And verily, We shall repay them in full their portion without diminution.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So be not thou in doubt concerning that which these (folk) worship. They worship only as their fathers worshipped aforetime. Lo! we shall pay them their whole due unabated.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So do not be in doubt about what these worship: they worship just as their fathers worshiped before, and We shall surely pay them their full share, undiminished.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Therefore do no be doubtful as to what these worship. They worship only that which their fathers worshiped before them. We shall indeed pay them in full their undiminished measure.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So do not be in doubt, [O Muhammad], as to what these [polytheists] are worshipping. They worship not except as their fathers worshipped before. And indeed, We will give them their share undiminished.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have no doubt that these people worship (idols). They worship what their fathers had worshipped before them. We will give them the exact recompense that they deserve.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore be not in doubt as to what these worship; they do not worship but as their fathers worshipped before; and most surely We will pay them back in full their portion undiminished.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have no doubt as to what they worship. They worship nothing but what their fathers worshipped before [them]. We shall certainly give them their share in full, without diminishing anything.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Be not then in doubt as to what these men worship. They worship nothing but what their fathers worshipped before (them): but verily We shall pay them back (in full) their portion without (the least) abatement.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fala taku fee miryatin mimma yaAAbudu haolai ma yaAAbudoona illa kama yaAAbudu abaohum min qablu wainna lamuwaffoohum naseebahum ghayra manqoosin

110.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ فَٱخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Verily We gave to Moses the Book, but there was disagreement about it. Had the decree of your Lord (delaying it) not been issued the matter would have been settled between them. They are still suspicious of it and in doubt.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed We gave Moosa the Book, hence there was discord regarding it; and were it not for a Word that had been previously passed from your Lord, the matter would have immediately been decided regarding them; and indeed they are in an intriguing doubt concerning it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We gave Moses the Book; and there was difference regarding it, and but for a word that preceded from thy Lord, it had been decided between them; and they are in doubt of it disquieting.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly vouchsafed unto Musa the Book, and disputation arose thereabout; and had not a word preceded from thy Lord, it would have been decreed between them. And verily they are concerning that in doubt disquieting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Indeed, We gave the Book to Musa (Moses), but differences arose therein, and had it not been for a Word that had gone forth before from your Lord, the case would have been judged between them, and indeed they are in grave doubt concerning it (this Quran).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We gave Moses the Scripture, but it was disputed. Were it not for a prior word from your Lord, it would have been settled between them. They are in serious doubt concerning it.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And We certainly gave Moses the Book before, and there arose disagreements about it (even as there are disagreements now about the Book revealed to you). Had it not been for a decree that had already gone forth from your Lord, the matter would have long been decided between them. Indeed they are in a disquieting doubt about it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Indeed, We gave the Book to Musa, but differences arose therein, and had it not been for a Word that had gone forth before from your Lord, the case would have been judged between them, and indeed they are in grave doubt concerning it (this Qur'an).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And we verily gave unto Moses the Scripture, and there was strife thereupon; and had it not been for a Word that had already gone forth from thy Lord, the case would have been judged between them, and lo! they are in grave doubt concerning it.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book, but differences arose about it, and were it not for a prior decree of your Lord, a decision would have been made between them; indeed they are in grave doubt concerning it.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We gave Moses the Book, but they differed regarding it, but for a word that preceded from your Lord, it had been decided between them; and they are indisquieting doubt of it.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture, but it came under disagreement. And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it would have been judged between them. And indeed they are, concerning the Qur'an, in disquieting doubt.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We gave the Book to Moses but people had different views about it. Had the Word of your Lord not been already ordained, He would have settled their differences (there and then). They are still in doubt about this.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly We gave the book to Musa, but it was gone against; and had not a word gone forth from your Lord, the matter would surely have been decided between them; and surely they are in a disquieting doubt about it.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We certainly gave the Book to Moses, but differences arose about it: had it not been that a word had gone forth before from your Lord, Judgement would indeed have been passed on them then and there: yet they are in grave doubt about it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We certainly gave the Book to Moses, but differences arose therein: had it not been that a word had gone forth before from thy Lord, the matter would have been decided between them, but they are in suspicious doubt concerning it.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad atayna moosa alkitaba faikhtulifa feehi walawla kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika laqudiya baynahum wainnahum lafee shakkin minhu mureebun

111.arabic: وَإِنَّ كُلًّا لَّمَّا لَيُوَفِّيَنَّهُمْ رَبُّكَ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely your Lord will reward everyone in accordance with his deeds. He is certainly aware of all they do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed to each and every one, your Lord will fully repay his deeds; He is Informed of their deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely each one of them -- thy Lord will pay them in full for their works; He is aware of the things they do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And verily unto each will thy Lord repay their works in full; verily of that which they work He is Aware.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And verily, to each of them your Lord will repay their works in full. Surely, He is All-Aware of what they do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Your Lord will repay each one of them in full for their deeds. He is Aware of everything they do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely your Lord will recompense all to the full for their deeds. For indeed He is well aware of all what they do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And verily, to each of them your Lord will repay their works in full. Surely, He is All-Aware of what they do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And lo! unto each thy Lord will verily repay his works in full. Lo! He is Informed of what they do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Your Lord will indeed recompense everyone fully for their works. Indeed, He is well aware of what they do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, your Lord will pay each one of them in full for their deeds. He is aware of the things they do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And indeed, each [of the believers and disbelievers] - your Lord will fully compensate them for their deeds. Indeed, He is Acquainted with what they do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God will certainly recompense everyone according to their deeds; He knows well all that you do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And your Lord will most surely pay back to all their deeds in full; surely He is aware of what they do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Your Lord will reward each one of them in full for their deeds. He is well-aware of all their actions.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And, of a surety, to all will your Lord pay back (in full the recompense) of their deeds: for He knoweth well all that they do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wainna kullan lamma layuwaffiyannahum rabbuka aAAmalahum innahu bima yaAAmaloona khabeerun

112.arabic: فَٱسْتَقِمْ كَمَآ أُمِرْتَ وَمَن تَابَ مَعَكَ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, you and those who turned to God with you, should walk along the straight path as you have been commanded, and do not transgress, for He verily sees whatsoever you do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore remain firm the way you are commanded to, and those who have turned along with you, and O people – do not rebel; indeed He is seeing your deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So go thou straight, as thou hast been commanded, and whoso repents with thee; and be you not insolent; surely He sees the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So stand thou straight as thou hast been commanded, thou and whosoever repented with thee; and be not arrogant; verily He is of that which ye work Beholder.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So stand (ask Allah to make) you (Muhammad SAW) firm and straight (on the religion of Islamic Monotheism) as you are commanded and those (your companions) who turn in repentance (unto Allah) with you, and transgress not (Allah's legal limits). Verily, He is All-Seer of what you do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So be upright, as you are commanded, along with those who repented with you, and do not transgress. He is Seeing of everything you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So remain, (O Muhammad), you and those who have returned with you (to the fold of faith and obedience from unbelief and rebellion) steadfast (in adhering to the straight way) as you were commanded. And do not exceed the limits of (service to Allah). For certainly He is aware of all what you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So stand (ask Allah to make) you firm and straight as you are commanded and those who turn in repentance with you, and transgress not. Verily, He is All-Seer of what you do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So tread thou the straight path as thou art commanded, and those who turn (unto Allah) with thee, and transgress not. Lo! He is Seer of what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So be steadfast, just as you have been commanded—[you] and whoever has turned [to Allah] with you—and do not overstep the bounds. Indeed, He watches what you do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So you and whosoever repents with you go straight as you have been commanded and do not be insolent, indeed, He sees what you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So remain on a right course as you have been commanded, [you] and those who have turned back with you [to Allah], and do not transgress. Indeed, He is Seeing of what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), be steadfast (in your faith) just as you have been commanded. Those who have turned to God in repentance with you, should also be steadfast in their faith. Do not indulge in rebellion. God is certainly aware of what you do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Continue then in the right way as you are commanded, as also he who has turned (to Allah) with you, and be not inordinate (O men!), surely He sees what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Therefore stand firm [in the straight path] as you are commanded, along with those who have turned to God with you, and do not exceed the bounds, for He sees everything you do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Therefore stand firm (in the straight Path) as thou art commanded,- thou and those who with thee turn (unto Allah); and transgress not (from the Path): for He seeth well all that ye do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faistaqim kama omirta waman taba maAAaka wala tatghaw innahu bima taAAmaloona baseerun

113.arabic: وَلَا تَرْكَنُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ فَتَمَسَّكُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not lean towards the wicked, or you will be caught in the flames of Hell, and have none to befriend you other than God, nor will you be given help.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And do not incline towards the unjust, for the fire will touch you – and you do not have any supporter other than Allah – then you will not be helped.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And lean not on the evildoers, so that the Fire touches you -- you have no protectors apart from God -- and then you will not be helped.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And lean not toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire should touch you, and ye have no protectors beside Allah nor ye would then be succoured.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And incline not toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire should touch you, and you have no protectors other than Allah, nor you would then be helped.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And do not incline towards those who do wrong, or the Fire may touch you; and you will have no protectors besides God, and you will not be saved.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And do not incline towards the wrong-doers lest the Fire might seize you and you will have none as your protector against Allah; and then you will not be helped from anywhere.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And incline not toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire should touch you, and you have no protectors other than Allah, nor would you then be helped.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And incline not toward those who do wrong lest the Fire touch you, and ye have no protecting friends against Allah, and afterward ye would not be helped.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not incline toward the wrongdoers, lest the Fire should touch you, and you will not have any friend besides Allah; then you will not be helped.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And do not incline to the wrongdoers lest you are touched by the Fire, for then you shall not be helped. You have no guardians other than Allah.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And do not incline toward those who do wrong, lest you be touched by the Fire, and you would not have other than Allah any protectors; then you would not be helped.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not be inclined towards the unjust ones lest you will be afflicted by the hell fire. Besides God, no one can be your protector nor will anyone be able to help you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And do not incline to those who are unjust, lest the fire touch you, and you have no guardians besides Allah, then you shall not be helped.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not incline toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire touch you. For [then] you would have none to protect you from God, and you will not be helped.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And incline not to those who do wrong, or the Fire will seize you; and ye have no protectors other than Allah, nor shall ye be helped.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala tarkanoo ila allatheena thalamoo fatamassakumu alnnaru wama lakum min dooni Allahi min awliyaa thumma la tunsaroona

114.arabic: وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ طَرَفَىِ ٱلنَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْحَسَنَـٰتِ يُذْهِبْنَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ذِكْرَىٰ لِلذَّٰكِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Stand up for the service of prayer at the two ends of day and the first watch of night. Remember that good deeds nullify the bad. This is a reminder for those who are observant.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And keep the prayer established at the two ends of the day and in some parts of the night; indeed good deeds wipe out the evil deeds; this is an advice to those who heed it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And perform the prayer at the two ends of the day and nigh of the night; surely the good deeds will drive away the evil deeds. That is a remembrance unto the mindful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And establish thou the prayer at the two ends of the day, and in the neighbouring watches of the night verily virtues take away vices. That is a reminder unto the mindful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), at the two ends of the day and in some hours of the night [i.e. the five compulsory Salat (prayers)]. Verily, the good deeds remove the evil deeds (i.e. small sins). That is a reminder (an advice) for the mindful (those who accept advice).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Perform the prayer at the borders of the day, and during the approaches of the night. The good deeds take away the bad deeds. This is a reminder for those who remember.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And establish the Prayer at the two ends of the day and in the first hours of the night. Indeed the good deeds drive away the evil deeds. This is a Reminder to those who are mindful of Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And perform the Salah, at the two ends of the day and in some hours of the night. Verily, the good deeds remove the evil deeds. That is a reminder for the mindful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Establish worship at the two ends of the day and in some watches of the night. Lo! good deeds annul ill-deeds. This is reminder for the mindful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Maintain the prayer at the two ends of the day, and during the early hours of the night. Indeed good deeds efface misdeeds. That is an admonition for the mindful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And establish your prayer at the two edges of the day and in part of the night. Good deeds will repel evil deeds. That is a remembrance for those whoremember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds do away with misdeeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say your prayers in the morning, the last portion of the day, and at the beginning of the night. Good deeds do away with the bad deeds. This is a reminder for those who take heed.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And keep up prayer in the two parts of the day and in the first hours of the night; surely good deeds take away evil deeds this is a reminder to the mindful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say your prayers morning and evening, and during parts of the night; surely good makes amends for evil. This is a reminder for people who pay heed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And establish regular prayers at the two ends of the day and at the approaches of the night: For those things, that are good remove those that are evil: Be that the word of remembrance to those who remember (their Lord):

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waaqimi alssalata tarafayi alnnahari wazulafan mina allayli inna alhasanati yuthhibna alssayyiati thalika thikra lilththakireena

115.arabic: وَٱصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Be steadfast, for verily God does not let the reward of those who are upright and do good to go waste.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And have patience, for Allah does not waste the wages of the righteous.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And be thou patient; God will not leave to waste the wage of the good-doers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And be patient thou; verily Allah wasteth not the hire of the welldoers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And be patient; verily, Allah loses not the reward of the good-doers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And be patient. God will not waste the reward of the virtuous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And be patient; for indeed Allah never lets the reward of those who do good go to waste.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And be patient; verily, Allah wastes not the reward of the doers of good.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And have patience, (O Muhammad), for lo! Allah loseth not the wages of the good.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And be patient; indeed Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Therefore have patience; Allah will not let the wage of the gooddoers go to waste.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And be patient, for indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Exercise patience; God does not ignore the reward of those who do good.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And be patient, for surely Allah does not waste the reward of the good-doers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Be steadfast; for surely, God does not let the wages of the righteous be wasted.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And be steadfast in patience; for verily Allah will not suffer the reward of the righteous to perish.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waisbir fainna Allaha la yudeeAAu ajra almuhsineena

116.arabic: فَلَوْلَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ أُو۟لُوا۟ بَقِيَّةٍ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْفَسَادِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّنْ أَنجَيْنَا مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَٱتَّبَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَآ أُتْرِفُوا۟ فِيهِ وَكَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If only there had been men endued with virtue in the ages before you, who could preserve men from doing evil in the world, other than the few We saved from among them. Those who were wicked followed that which made them dissolute, and became sinners.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So why were not there some people, from the generations before you, who had some goodness remaining in them in order to prevent (others) from causing turmoil in the earth? Except a few among them – the very ones whom We had rescued; and the unjust remained pursuing what they were given, and they were guilty.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or if there had been, of the generations before you, men of a remainder forbidding corruption in the earth -- except a few of those whom We delivered of them; but the evildoers followed the ease they were given to exult in and became sinners.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Why were there not of the generations before you owner of wisdom restraining others from corruption on the earth, except a few of those whom We delivered from amongst them! And those who did wrong followed that in which they luxuriated, and they had been sinners.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: If only there had been among the generations before you, persons having wisdom, prohibiting (others) from Al-Fasad (disbelief, polytheism, and all kinds of crimes and sins) in the earth, except a few of those whom We saved from among them. Those who did wrong pursued the enjoyment of good things of (this worldly) life, and were Mujrimun (criminals, disbelievers in Allah, polytheists, sinners, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If only there were, among the generations before you, people with wisdom, who spoke against corruption on earth—except for the few whom We saved. But the wrongdoers pursued the luxuries they were indulged in, and thus became guilty.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Why were there not, out of the generations that passed away before you, righteous men who would forbid others from causing corruption on the earth? And if such were there, they were only a few whom We had saved from those generations, or else the wrong-doers kept pursuing the ease and comfort which had been conferred upon them, thus losing themselves in sinfulness.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: If only there had been among the generations before you persons having wisdom, prohibiting (others) from Fasad (corruption) in the earth, except a few of those whom We saved from among them! Those who did wrong pursued the enjoyment of good things of (this worldly) life, and were criminals.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If only there had been among the generations before you men possessing a remnant (of good sense) to warn (their people) from corruption in the earth, as did a few of those whom We saved from them! The wrong-doers followed that by which they were made sapless, and were guilty.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Why were there not among the generations before you a remnant [of the wise] who might forbid corruption in the earth, except a few of those whom We delivered from among them? Those who were wrongdoers pursued that in which they had been granted affluence, and they were a guilty lot.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If (only) there had been except for a few among them whom We saved among the generations that have gone before you, some that remained forbidding corruption in the land, but the wrongdoers pursued the ease they had been given to delight in and became sinners.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So why were there not among the generations before you those of enduring discrimination forbidding corruption on earth - except a few of those We saved from among them? But those who wronged pursued what luxury they were given therein, and they were criminals.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Why were there no people of understanding among those people of the destroyed towns of the past except for a few, whom we saved from destruction, to prevent people from committing evil in the land? The unjust among them indulged in worldly pleasures and so became guilty.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But why were there not among the generations before you those possessing understanding, who should have forbidden the making of mischief in the earth, except a few of those whom We delivered from among them? And those who were unjust went after what they are made to enjoy of plenty, and they were guilty.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Why, then, were there not among the generations before you upright men who would speak out against the [spread of] corruption on earth -- except for the few whom We saved? But the wrongdoers pursued their worldly pleasures and thus became guilty.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Why were there not, among the generations before you, persons possessed of balanced good sense, prohibiting (men) from mischief in the earth - except a few among them whom We saved (from harm)? But the wrong-doers pursued the enjoyment of the good things of life which were given them, and persisted in sin.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falawla kana mina alqurooni min qablikum oloo baqiyyatin yanhawna AAani alfasadi fee alardi illa qaleelan mimman anjayna minhum waittabaAAa allatheena thalamoo ma otrifoo feehi wakanoo mujrimeena

117.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ لِيُهْلِكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُهَا مُصْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Your Lord would not surely destroy unjustly human habitations so long as the people are righteous.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And your Lord is not such as to destroy townships without reason, while their people are righteous.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet thy Lord would never destroy the cities unjustly, while as yet their people were putting things right.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And thy Lord is not one to destroy cities wrongously while the inhabitants thereof are rectifiers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And your Lord would never destroy the towns wrongfully, while their people were right-doers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Your Lord would never destroy the towns wrongfully, while their inhabitants are righteous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And your Lord is not such as would wrongfully destroy human habitations while their inhabitants are righteous.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And your Lord would never destroy the towns wrongfully, while their people were doers of good.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: In truth thy Lord destroyed not the townships tyrannously while their folk were doing right.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Your Lord would never destroy the townships unjustly while their inhabitants were bringing about reform.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Your Lord would never destroy the villages unjustly, whilst their people were reforming.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Your Lord would not have destroyed those people (of the towns) for their injustice if they had tried to reform themselves.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And it did not beseem your Lord to have destroyed the towns tyrannously, while their people acted well.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Your Lord would never unjustly destroy communities while their people were trying to reform.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor would thy Lord be the One to destroy communities for a single wrong-doing, if its members were likely to mend.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama kana rabbuka liyuhlika alqura bithulmin waahluha muslihoona

118.arabic: وَلَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّكَ لَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً ۖ وَلَا يَزَالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But if your Lord had pleased He could have made all human beings into one community of belief. But they would still have differed from one another,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had your Lord willed, He would have made mankind one nation – and they will always keep differing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Had thy Lord willed, He would have made mankind one nation; but they continue in their differences

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had thy Lord Willed, He would surely have made mankind of one community, and they will not cease differing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if your Lord had so willed, He could surely have made mankind one Ummah [nation or community (following one religion only i.e. Islam)], but they will not cease to disagree,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had your Lord willed, He could have made humanity one community, but they continue to differ.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had your Lord so willed, He would surely have made mankind one community. But as things stand, now they will not cease to differ among themselves and to follow erroneous ways

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if your Lord had so willed, He could surely have made mankind one Ummah, but they will not cease to disagree.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if thy Lord had willed, He verily would have made mankind one nation, yet they cease not differing,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had your Lord wished, He would have made mankind one community; but they continue to differ,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had your Lord willed, He would have made mankind a single nation. But they continue in their differences

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if your Lord had willed, He could have made mankind one community; but they will not cease to differ.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had your Lord wanted, He would have made all people one united nation. They still have different beliefs

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if your Lord had pleased He would certainly have made people a single nation, and they shall continue to differ.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If your Lord had wished, He would have made mankind into one community. As it is, they will not cease to dispute, --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If thy Lord had so willed, He could have made mankind one people: but they will not cease to dispute.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw shaa rabbuka lajaAAala alnnasa ommatan wahidatan wala yazaloona mukhtalifeena

119.arabic: إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ رَبُّكَ ۚ وَلِذَٰلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ۗ وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Except those on whom your Lord had mercy for which He has created them. But fulfilled shall be the word of your Lord. "I will fill up Hell with jinns and men."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Except those upon whom your Lord had mercy; and this is why He created them; and the Word of your Lord has been concluded that, “Indeed I will, surely, fill hell with jinns and men combined.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: excepting those on whom thy Lord has mercy. To that end He created them, and perfectly is fulfilled the word of thy Lord: 'I shall assuredly fill Gehenna with jinn and men all together.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Save these on whom thy Lord hath mercy; and for that he hath created them. And fulfilled will be the word of thy Lord: surely I will fill Hell with the jinn and mankind together.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Except him on whom your Lord has bestowed His Mercy (the follower of truth - Islamic Monotheism) and for that did He create them. And the Word of your Lord has been fulfilled (i.e. His Saying): "Surely, I shall fill Hell with jinns and men all together."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Except those on whom your Lord has mercy—for that reason He created them. The Word of your Lord is final: “I will fill Hell with jinn and humans, altogether.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: except for those on whom your Lord has mercy. And it is for this (exercise of freedom of choice) that He has created them. And the word of your Lord was fulfilled: 'Indeed I will fill the Hell, with men and jinn, altogether.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Except him on whom your Lord has bestowed His mercy, and for that did He create them. And the Word of your Lord has been fulfilled (His saying): "Surely, I shall fill Hell with Jinn and men all together."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Save him on whom thy Lord hath mercy; and for that He did create them. And the Word of thy Lord hath been fulfilled: Verily I shall fill hell with the jinn and mankind together.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: except those on whom your Lord has mercy—and that is why He created them—and the word of your Lord has been fulfilled: ‘I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans, all together!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: except those on whom your Lord shows mercy. For this end He has created them. The Word of your Lord shall be perfectly completed: 'I shall fillGehenna (Hell) with jinn and people, all together'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Except whom your Lord has given mercy, and for that He created them. But the word of your Lord is to be fulfilled that, "I will surely fill Hell with jinn and men all together."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: except those upon whom God has granted His mercy. God has created them to receive mercy. The decree of your Lord that He will fill hell with both jinn and human beings has already been ordained.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Except those on whom your Lord has mercy; and for this did He create them; and the word of your Lord is fulfilled: Certainly I will fill hell with the jinn and the men, all together.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and to this end He has created them [all], except for those to whom your Lord has shown mercy. The word of your Lord shall be fulfilled, "I will fill Hell with jinn and men all together."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Except those on whom thy Lord hath bestowed His Mercy: and for this did He create them: and the Word of thy Lord shall be fulfilled: "I will fill Hell with jinns and men all together."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Illa man rahima rabbuka walithalika khalaqahum watammat kalimatu rabbika laamlaanna jahannama mina aljinnati waalnnasi ajmaAAeena

120.arabic: وَكُلًّا نَّقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلرُّسُلِ مَا نُثَبِّتُ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَ ۚ وَجَآءَكَ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَقُّ وَمَوْعِظَةٌ وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The histories of apostles that We relate to you are (meant) to strengthen your heart. Through them has the truth come to you, and guidance, and reminder to those who believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We relate to you all the accounts of Noble Messengers, in order to steady your heart with it; and in this the truth has come to you, and for the Muslims a preaching and advice.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And all that We relate to thee of the tidings of the Messengers is that whereby We strengthen thy heart; in these there has come to thee the truth and an admonition, and a reminder to the believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And all that We recount unto thee of the stories of the apostles is in order that We may make firm thy heart thereby. And in this there hath come to thee truth and an exhortation and an admonition unto the believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And all that We relate to you (O Muhammad SAW) of the news of the Messengers is in order that We may make strong and firm your heart thereby. And in this (chapter of the Quran) has come to you the truth, as well as an admonition and a reminder for the believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Everything We narrate to you of the history of the messengers is to strengthen your heart therewith. The truth has come to you in this, and a lesson, and a reminder for the believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Muhammad!) We narrate these anecdotes of Messengers to you that We may strengthen through them your heart. In these anecdotes come to you the truth, and an exhortation, and a reminder for the believers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And all that We relate to you of the news of the Messengers is in order that We may make strong and firm your heart thereby. And in this has come to you the truth, as well as an admonition and a reminder for the believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And all that We relate unto thee of the story of the messengers is in order that thereby We may make firm thy heart. And herein hath come unto thee the Truth and an exhortation and a reminder for believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whatever We relate to you of the accounts of the apostles are those by which We strengthen your heart, and there has come to you in this [surah] the truth and an advice and admonition for the faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And all We relate to you of the tidings of the Messengers is that whereby We strengthen your heart and through these the truth has come to you, and anadmonition and a reminder to the believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And each [story] We relate to you from the news of the messengers is that by which We make firm your heart. And there has come to you, in this, the truth and an instruction and a reminder for the believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), We tell you all the stories of the Messengers which will strengthen your heart. In the Quran We have revealed the Truth to you with good advice and reminders for the faithful ones.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And all we relate to you of the accounts of the apostles is to strengthen your heart therewith; and in this has come to you the truth and an admonition, and a reminder to the believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We have told you the stories of the prophets to make your heart firm and in these accounts truth has come to you, with an exhortation and a reminder for the believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: All that we relate to thee of the stories of the messengers,- with it We make firm thy heart: in them there cometh to thee the Truth, as well as an exhortation and a message of remembrance to those who believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wakullan naqussu AAalayka min anbai alrrusuli ma nuthabbitu bihi fuadaka wajaaka fee hathihi alhaqqu wamawAAithatun wathikra lilmumineena

121.arabic: وَقُل لِّلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنَّا عَـٰمِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say to the infidels. "Act as best you can, we are acting too;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And say to the disbelievers, “Keep on with your works in your places; we are carrying out ours.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And say to the unbelievers: 'Act you according to your station; we are acting.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And say thou unto those who believe not: work according to your condition verily We, going to work in our way.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And say to those who do not believe: "Act according to your ability and way, We are acting (in our way).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And say to those who do not believe, “Act according to your ability; and so will we.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those who are bent on not believing, tell them: 'Work according to your way and we are working according to our way.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And say to those who do not believe: "Act according to your ability and way, We are acting.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And say unto those who believe not: Act according to your power. Lo! We (too) are acting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say to those who do not have faith, ‘Act according to your ability; we too are acting.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say to those who disbelieve: 'Do according to your station; we are doing according to our station'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And say to those who do not believe, "Work according to your position; indeed, we are working.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Tell the unbelievers, "Do as you wish and I will do as I believe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And say to those who do not believe: Act according to your state; surely we too are acting.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say to those who do not believe, you do things in your way and we are doing things in our way,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say to those who do not believe: "Do what ever ye can: We shall do our part;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqul lillatheena la yuminoona iAAmaloo AAala makanatikum inna AAamiloona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And wait (for what is to come), we are also waiting (to see)."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And wait – we too are waiting.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And watch and wait; we are also watching and waiting.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And await; as verily we are awaiting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you wait! We (too) are waiting."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “And wait; we too are waiting.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And do wait for the end of things; we too are waiting.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you wait! We (too) are waiting."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And wait! Lo! We (too) are waiting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And wait! We too are waiting.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Wait; we too are waiting'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And wait, indeed, we are waiting."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Wait, and I, too, will be waiting with you."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And wait; surely we are waiting also.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and wait, we too are waiting."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And wait ye! We too shall wait."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waintathiroo inna muntathiroona

123.arabic: وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُ ٱلْأَمْرُ كُلُّهُۥ فَٱعْبُدْهُ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَيْهِ ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: To God belong the secrets of the heavens and the earth, and all things will go back to Him. So worship Him and put your trust in Him; your Lord is not heedless of what you do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And for Allah only are the hidden of the heavens and of the earth, and towards Him only is the return of all matters – therefore worship Him and trust Him; and your Lord is not unaware of what you do.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: To God belongs the Unseen in the heavens and the earth. To Him the whole matter shall be returned; so serve Him, and put thy trust in Him. Thy Lord is not heedless of the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Allah's is the Unseen of the heavens and the earth, and unto Him the whole affair shall be brought back. So worship Him thou and rely on Him; and thy Lord is not negligent of that which ye work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to Allah belongs the Ghaib (unseen) of the heavens and the earth, and to Him return all affairs (for decision). So worship Him (O Muhammad SAW) and put your trust in Him. And your Lord is not unaware of what you (people) do."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To God belongs the future of the heavens and the earth, and to Him all authority goes back. So worship Him, and rely on Him. Your Lord is never unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: All that is hidden in the heavens and the earth lies within the power of Allah. To Him are all matters referred for judgement. So do serve Him, and place in Him all your trust. Your Lord is not heedless of what you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to Allah belongs the Ghayb of the heavens and the earth, and to Him return all affairs (for decision). So worship Him and put your trust in Him. And your Lord is not unaware of what you (people) do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Allah's is the Invisible of the heavens and the earth, and unto Him the whole matter will be returned. So worship Him and put thy trust in Him. Lo! thy Lord is not unaware of what ye (mortals) do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: To Allah belongs the Unseen of the heavens and the earth, and to Him all matters are returned. So worship Him and trust in Him. Your Lord is not oblivious of what you do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: To Allah belongs the unseen in the heavens and in the earth; to Him the matter, in its entirety, shall be returned. Worship Him, and put your trust in Him. Your Lord is not inattentive of the things you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to Allah belong the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth and to Him will be returned the matter, all of it, so worship Him and rely upon Him. And your Lord is not unaware of that which you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To God belongs the knowledge of the unseen in the heavens and the earth and to Him do all affairs return. Worship Him and trust Him. Your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Allah's is the unseen in the heavens and the earth, and to Him is returned the whole of the affair; therefore serve Him and rely on Him, and your Lord is not heedless of what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The knowledge of the secret of the heavens and the earth belongs to God alone, and to Him shall all affairs be referred. So worship Him and put your trust in Him alone. Your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To Allah do belong the unseen (secrets) of the heavens and the earth, and to Him goeth back every affair (for decision): then worship Him, and put thy trust in Him: and thy Lord is not unmindful of aught that ye do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walillahi ghaybu alssamawati waalardi wailayhi yurjaAAu alamru kulluhu faoAAbudhu watawakkal AAalayhi wama rabbuka bighafilin AAamma taAAmaloona
